When he moved to Cambridge and began his active work at Harvard, he took up his residence in the historic Craigie House, overlooking the Charles River-a house in which Washington had been quartered for some months when in 1775 he took command of the Continental army.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 大学毕业后，他在国外待了几年，回到美国后，先在鲍登学院任教，后来又到哈佛大学任教。迁居剑桥、在哈佛正式执教之后，他住进了俯瞰查尔斯河的历史名宅克雷吉别墅——1775 年华盛顿出任大陆军统帅时，曾在这座宅子里驻扎过几个月。

paragraphs.text: Longfellow is the poet who has spoken most sincerely and sympathetically to the hearts of the common people and to children. His style is notable for its simplicity and grace. His Hiawatha is a national poem that records the picturesque traditions of the American Indian. Its charm and melody are the delight of all children, and in years to come, when the race which it describes has utterly disappeared, we shall value at even higher worth these stories of the romantic past of America and of the brave people who inhabited these mountains and plains before the white man came.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 朗费罗这位诗人，最真诚、最满怀同情地向普通民众和孩子们的心灵倾诉。他的诗风以质朴优雅著称。他的《海华沙》是一首民族史诗，记录了美洲印第安人富有诗意的种种传统。它的魅力与韵律令所有孩子着迷；将来有一天，当它所描写的那个民族彻底消逝时，我们会更加珍视这些关于美国浪漫往昔的故事，珍视那些在白人到来之前世代生活在这片山岭与平原上的勇敢民族。

paragraphs.text: Besides Indian stories, Longfellow wrote many narratives in verse dealing with old legends of America. "The Skeleton in Armor" is an example of the legends about European explorers who came here before the days of Columbus. Evangehne and The Courtship of Miles Standish are longer poems which find their subjects in early colonial history. He wrote also of legends of Europe, and was well acquainted with stories and romances of older civilizations than ours. Equally well-known poems, of a different type, are those in which household joys and sorrows give the theme. Longfellow is the poet of the home-life, of simple hopes, of true religious faith. His spirit was the Spirit of a child, affectionate, loyal, eager for romance and knightly adventure. He is the "Children's Poet," as the poem "The Children's Hour" helps to show. There were sorrows as well as joys in his life, and this is why we go to him in trouble and why so many people know his poems by heart. Sorrow never took away his faith or made him bitter. He is genial and kindly, the friend--of all Americans everywhere.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 除了印第安故事，朗费罗还写过许多取材于美国古老传说的叙事诗。《铠甲骷髅》就是一例，讲的是哥伦布之前来到这片土地的欧洲探险者的传说。《伊凡杰琳》和《迈尔斯·斯坦迪什的求婚》是两首较长的诗，取材于早期殖民地历史。他也写欧洲的传说，对比我们更古老的那些文明的故事与传奇了如指掌。另有一类同样脍炙人口、风格迥异的诗，则以家庭的悲欢为主题。朗费罗是歌咏家庭生活、朴素愿望和虔诚信仰的诗人。他怀着一颗赤子之心，深情、忠诚，渴望传奇与骑士般的冒险。他是“儿童诗人”，《孩子们的时光》一诗便是明证。他的一生既有欢乐也有悲伤，正因如此，我们在困顿时会去读他的诗，也因此有那么多人能背诵他的诗篇。悲伤从未夺走他的信仰，也不曾让他变得苦涩。他亲切和善，是天下所有美国人的朋友。

entries.title: The Children's Hour, Volume 3: Christmas Peace

paragraphs.text: That sweetly prophetic evening silence, before the great feast of Good-Will, does not come over everything each year, even in a lonely cottage on an abandoned farm in Connecticut, than which you cannot possibly imagine anything more silent or more remote from the noise of the world. Sometimes it rains in torrents just on that night, sometimes it blows a raging gale that twists the leafless birches and elms and hickory trees like dry grass and bends the dark firs and spruces as if they were feathers, and you can hardly be heard unless you shout, for the howling and screaming and whistling of the blast.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 在友爱的盛大节日来临前夕，那种甜美而预示着佳节的夜晚静谧，并非年年都会笼罩万物——哪怕是在康涅狄格州一处废弃农场的孤零小屋里也是如此，而你再难想象有什么地方比这里更寂静、更远离尘世的喧嚣。有时偏偏就在那一夜大雨倾盆，有时则狂风大作，把光秃秃的桦树、榆树和山核桃树像干草般拧来扭去，把深色的冷杉和云杉压弯得仿佛轻飘飘的羽毛，狂风呼啸、尖啸、呜咽，你不喊上一嗓子几乎没人听得见。

paragraphs.text: But now and then, once in four or five years perhaps, the feathery snow lies a foot deep, fresh-fallen, on the still country side and in the woods; and the waxing moon sheds her large light on all, and Nature holds her breath to wait for the happy day and tries to sleep, but cannot from sheer happiness and peace. Indoors, the fire is glowing on the wide hearth, a great bed of coals that will last all night and be enough, because it is not bitter weather, but only cold and clear and still, as it should be; or if there is only a poor stove, the iron door is open and a comfortable, cheery red light shines out from within upon the battered iron plate and the wooden floor beyond; and the older people sit around it, not saying much, and thinking with their hearts rather than with their heads; but small boys and girls know that interesting things have been happening in the kitchen all the afternoon, and are rather glad that the supper was not very good, because there will be more room for good things to-morrow; and the grown-ups and the children have made up any little differences of opinion they may have had before supper time, because Good-Will must reign, and reign alone, like Alexander; so that there is nothing at all to regret, and nothing hurts anybody any more, and they are all happy in just waiting for King Christmas to open the door softly and make them all great people in his kingdom. But if it is the right sort of house, he is already looking in through the window, to be sure that every one is all ready for him, and that nothing has been forgotten.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 但偶尔，也许每四五年才有一回，蓬松的雪花新落下来，在寂静的乡间和林中积起一尺来深；渐盈的月亮把皎洁的清辉洒向万物，大自然屏息等待那幸福的一天，想要入睡，却因满心的幸福与安宁而无法成眠。屋内，宽大的壁炉里炉火熊熊，厚厚一层炭火足以燃烧整夜，也尽够用了，因为天气并非严寒刺骨，而只是清冷、晴朗而宁静，本该如此；倘若家里只有一只简陋的火炉，那铁炉门也敞开着，从里面透出舒适欢快的红光，映照在斑驳的铁板和那头的木地板上；年长的人围炉而坐，话不多说，用心而非用脑去思量；可小男孩小女孩们却晓得，整个下午厨房里都在忙活着有趣的事，还暗自庆幸晚饭不太丰盛，因为明天的肚子好给美食腾出更多地方；大人和孩子也都把晚饭前那点小小的意见分歧消弭了，因为友爱必须当家，而且要像亚历山大大帝那样独自称王；于是再没有什么可遗憾的，再没有什么能伤着谁，他们个个心满意足，只等圣诞之王轻轻推门而入，让他们都成为他国度里的大人物。可若这屋子合他的心意，他其实早已透过窗子往里张望，要确认人人都为他备齐了一切，没有遗漏任何事情。

paragraphs.text: No other visitor is allowed to enter the house before the "Polaznik," the Christmas guest, has come. The part is usually taken by some boy from a neighbor's family, who comes very early and brings with him a woollen glove full of wheat. When at his knock the door is opened, he showers the wheat over those around the brightly burning fire and into all corners of the room with the greeting, "Christ is born." The mother of the family throws a handful of wheat at him and all the others shout, "In truth, He is born!" The guest then walks straight to the fire, and with the heavy shovel strikes the burning log with all his force repeatedly, so that thousands of sparks rise high in the chimney, while he says, "May you have this year so many oxen, so many horses, so many sheep, so many pigs, so many beehives of honey, so much good luck, so much success and happiness." After this good wish he kisses his host, drops to his knees before the Christmas log on the fire, kisses one end of it, which sticks out of the fireplace into the room, and places a coin upon it as his gift. As he rises, a woman offers him a low wooden chair, but just as he seats himself draws it away so that he sits down hard upon the ground, and is thus supposed to fix to it firmly every good wish he has spoken. Finally he is wrapped in a thick blanket, and with it around him sits quietly for a few minutes while the young folks who are to tend the flocks and herds in the coming year come to the hearth and kiss each other solemnly across the Christmas log. The wearing of the blanket is said to insure thick cream in the next year, and the shepherds' kisses will make for peace and plenty among the cattle.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 在被称作“波拉兹尼克”的圣诞贵客到来之前,不许其他任何客人进屋。这个角色通常由邻家的一个男孩扮演,他一大早就来,还带着一只装满麦粒的羊毛手套。他一敲门,门便打开,他就把麦粒撒向围在熊熊炉火旁的众人,撒向屋子的每一个角落,口中道贺:“基督诞生了。”女主人也抓起一把麦粒朝他撒去,其余的人齐声高呼:“他真的诞生了!”客人随即径直走到火边,抡起沉重的铁铲,使出浑身力气一下下敲打燃烧的木柴,顿时千万点火星腾上烟囱;他一边敲一边念道:“愿你今年牛成群、马成群、羊成群、猪成群,蜂蜜满箱,好运连连,事事顺遂,幸福美满。”道完这番祝福,他亲吻主人,在炉火上的圣诞木柴前跪下,亲吻那根伸出壁炉、探进屋里的一头,并在上面放一枚硬币作为礼物。他起身时,一位妇人递给他一把矮木椅,可就在他要坐下的当口又把椅子抽走,让他重重地跌坐在地上——据说这样就能把他说过的每一句祝福都牢牢地定住。最后,人们用一条厚毯子把他裹起来,他便裹着毯子静静地坐上几分钟;与此同时,来年要照看牛羊的年轻人来到炉边,隔着圣诞木柴庄重地互相亲吻。据说裹这条毯子能保来年奶油浓稠,而牧人们的亲吻则会给牲畜带来安宁与兴旺。

paragraphs.text: Before the chief meal of the day, all the members of the family gather about its head, each with a lighted candle in hand, while he prays briefly. Then they turn and kiss each other with such greetings as: "Peace of God be with us!" "Christos is born!" "In truth, He is born!" "Therefore let us bow before Christos and His birth." And toward the end of the meal all stand to drink "to the glory of God and of the birth of Christ," which marks the end of the Christmas celebration.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 在一天的正餐之前,全家人围拢在家长身边,每人手里擎着一支点燃的蜡烛,家长则做简短的祷告。然后他们转过身互相亲吻,彼此道贺:“愿上帝的平安与我们同在!”“基督诞生了!”“他真的诞生了!”“因此,让我们朝拜基督和他的降生吧。”临近用餐结束时,众人一齐起立举杯,“为上帝的荣耀、为基督的诞生”干杯——这便标志着圣诞庆典的结束。

entries.title: For the Children's Hour: 4. LOUISA MAY ALCOTT, AUTHOR

paragraphs.text: Louisa May Alcott was born in Germantown, Pa., November 29, 1832. Her father was a cultured school-teacher and her mother of an old aristocratic family. Louisa was the eldest of four daughters, whose happy life she pictures in "Little Women," herself being "Jo." She was a wild, happy-hearted, enthusiastic girl, preferring whistling and romping and boys' games rather than girls'. Their home was frequently visited by such literary people as Emerson, Thoreau, and Hawthorne, who were her father's friends. At eight years of age she wrote this poem of eight lines:
paragraphs.translation_zh: 路易莎·梅·奥尔科特于1832年11月29日出生在宾夕法尼亚州的日耳曼敦。她的父亲是一位有教养的教师，母亲则出身于一个古老的贵族世家。路易莎是四姐妹中的长女，她在《小妇人》里描绘了姐妹们幸福的生活，自己就是书中的“乔”。她是个野性十足、生性快活、热情洋溢的女孩，比起女孩子的游戏，她更爱吹口哨、撒欢打闹和玩男孩子的游戏。爱默生、梭罗、霍桑这些文学家都是她父亲的朋友，常来家中做客。八岁那年，她写下了这首八行小诗：

paragraphs.text: Welcome, welcome, little stranger, Fear no harm and fear no danger; We are glad to see you here, For you sing, "Sweet spring is near." Now the white snow melts away; Now the flowers blossom gay. Come, dear bird, and build your nest For we love our robin best.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 欢迎呀，欢迎，小小的远客，莫怕伤害，也莫怕危险；我们真高兴见你来到这里，因为你歌唱着：“甜美的春天近了。”如今白雪渐渐消融；如今繁花欢欣绽放。来吧，亲爱的鸟儿，筑起你的巢，因为我们最爱我们的知更鸟。

paragraphs.text: Her mother preserved this poem, and told her if she kept on she might one day be a second Shakespeare. She was fond of telling fairy stories to amuse her sisters and friends, and often turned the old tales into little plays which the children acted in a barn. One of these plays was "Jack and the Bean Stalk." A squash vine, placed in the barn, was the bean-stalk, and when it was cut down the boy who played Giant, would come tumbling down from the hay-loft. At thirteen she wrote the beautiful poem, "My Kingdom." After she became a school-teacher she was always helping somebody, taking care of an invalid or the poor, or sewing to help her mother. She continued to write stories. Some of the stories were rejected and the publisher advised her to stick to her school-teaching. Returning from the Civil War, where she had been a valued nurse to the wounded soldiers, she presented, through her father, several short stories to a publisher, who rejected them, with the advice that she write a story for girls. She thought she could not do that, and wrote "Little Women" to prove that she could not, but it is perhaps the best-loved girls' story ever written. Then she wrote "Little Men," of which fifty thousand copies were ordered before it was printed. She received one hundred thousand dollars for her books. Her life-desire was now realized in having money enough to make her family comfortable. Her father died in 1888, and she followed him only three days after. Miss Louisa May Alcott, besides being a writer, was also an earnest advocate of woman suffrage and temperance.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 她的母亲保存了这首诗，并告诉她，只要坚持写下去，有朝一日或许能成为第二个莎士比亚。她喜欢讲童话故事逗姐妹和朋友们开心，还常把古老的传说改编成小短剧，让孩子们在谷仓里上演。其中一出戏是《杰克与魔豆》。谷仓里立着的一根南瓜藤就权当那株豆茎，藤一被砍倒，扮演巨人的男孩便从干草棚顶翻滚下来。十三岁时，她写下了优美的诗篇《我的王国》。当上教师以后，她总在帮助别人——照料病人或穷人，或是做针线活贴补母亲。她笔耕不辍。有些故事遭到退稿，出版商劝她还是安心教书。她曾在内战中担任伤兵护士，深受倚重；战后归来，她托父亲向一位出版商递交了几篇短篇小说，对方退了稿，建议她写一个给女孩看的故事。她原以为自己写不来，便写《小妇人》来证明自己确实写不出，不料它或许成了有史以来最受喜爱的少女小说。随后她又写了《小男人》，此书还没付印就被预订出五万册。这些书为她带来了十万美元的收入。至此，她毕生的心愿终于实现——攒下了足够的钱，让一家人过上舒适的日子。她的父亲于1888年去世，仅仅三天之后，她也随之离世。路易莎·梅·奥尔科特小姐不仅是一位作家，还是妇女选举权与禁酒运动的热忱倡导者。

entries.title: The Blue Fairy Book: The Blinded Giant

paragraphs.text: At Dalton, near Thirsk, in Yorkshire, there is a mill. It has quite recently been rebuilt; but when I was at Dalton, six years ago, the old building stood. In front of the house was a long mound which went by the name of "the giant's grave," and in the mill you can see a long blade of iron something like a scythe-blade, but not curved, which was called "the giant's knife," because of a very curious story which is told of this knife. Would you like to hear it? Well, it isn't very long.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 在约克郡瑟斯克附近的道尔顿，有一座磨坊。它最近刚刚重建过；可六年前我在道尔顿的时候，那座老房子还在。屋子前面有一道长长的土堆，人称“巨人之墓”；磨坊里还能看到一把长长的铁刃，有点像镰刀片，却是直的，被叫作“巨人之刀”，因为这把刀有一段非常离奇的来历。你想听听吗？放心，故事不长。

paragraphs.text: There once lived a giant at this mill who had only one eye in the middle of his forehead, and he ground men's bones to make his bread. One day he captured on Pilmoor a lad named Jack, and instead of grinding him in the mill he kept him grinding as his servant, and never let him get away. Jack served the giant seven years, and never was allowed a holiday the whole time. At last he could bear it no longer. Topcliffe fair was coming on, and Jack begged that he might be allowed to go there.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 从前这座磨坊里住着一个巨人，额头正中只长着一只眼睛，他把人骨磨碎了做面包吃。一天，他在皮尔莫尔抓住了一个名叫杰克的小伙子，没有把他磨成粉，而是留下他当仆人推磨干活，再不许他逃走。杰克给巨人干了七年，自始至终没歇过一天假。终于他再也忍受不下去了。托普克利夫集市快到了，杰克央求让他去赶集。

paragraphs.text: "I've been grinding and grinding these seven years," said Jack, "and not a holiday have I had. I'll have one now, whatever you say."
paragraphs.translation_zh: “我推磨推了整整七年，”杰克说，“一天假都没歇过。这回不管你怎么说，我都要歇上一天。”

paragraphs.text: Well, the day was hot, and after dinner the giant lay down in the mill with his head on a sack and dozed. He had been eating in the mill, and had laid down a great loaf of bone bread by his side, and the knife I told you about was in his hand, but his fingers relaxed their hold of it in sleep. Jack seized the knife, and holding it with both his hands drove the blade into the single eye of the giant, who woke with a howl of agony, and starting up, barred the door. Jack was again in difficulties, for he couldn't get out, but he soon found a way out of them. The giant had a favourite dog, which had also been sleeping when his master was blinded. So Jack killed the dog, skinned it, and threw the hide over his back.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 那天天气很热，吃过午饭，巨人就在磨坊里躺下，头枕着一只麻袋打起盹来。他一直在磨坊里吃东西，身边搁着一大条骨头面包，我先前说的那把刀就握在他手里，可睡着睡着，手指便松开了。杰克一把抓起刀，双手攥紧，朝巨人那只独眼狠狠刺了进去。巨人疼得嚎叫着惊醒，猛地跳起来把门闩上了。杰克又犯了难，出不去了，但很快想出了办法。巨人有一只心爱的狗，主人被刺瞎时它也正睡着。杰克便把狗杀了、剥了皮，把狗皮披在背上。

paragraphs.text: "At him, Truncheon," said the giant; "at the little wretch that I've fed these seven years, and now has blinded me."
paragraphs.translation_zh: “上啊，特伦琼！”巨人喊道，“咬那个我养了七年、如今却把我弄瞎的小坏蛋！”

paragraphs.text: "Bow, wow," says Jack, and ran between the giant's legs on all-fours, barking till he got to the door. He unlatched it and was off, and never more was seen at Dalton Mill.
paragraphs.translation_zh: “汪，汪，”杰克学着狗叫，四肢着地从巨人两腿间钻了过去，一路汪汪叫到门口。他拨开门闩就溜了，从此再没人在道尔顿磨坊见过他。

entries.title: For the Children's Hour: 16. THE CRIPPLE AT THE BEAUTIFUL GATE

paragraphs.text: One afternoon two friends were walking along a street in Jerusalem on their way to the evening sacrifice in the temple. At one of the entrances--the Beautiful Gate (so named because of its snow-white marble steps leading up to its great door of costly brass)--sat a poor lame man, begging. His feet and ankles were so crippled that he had never been able to walk or even to stand. His friends carried this helpless cripple and laid him every morning at this temple entrance to beg charity from those who went to pray. As soon as the man saw these two friends, Peter and John, he cried piteously, "Give charity!" Standing still and looking him quietly in the eye, the two friends said, "Look on us!" He looked up at once most expectantly. Peter said, "Silver and gold have I none, but what I have, I give thee. In the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, walk!" Taking him by the hand Peter lifted him up, and immediately his feet and ankle-bones became strong. The man leaped up and went into the temple with Peter and John, walking and leaping and praising God. All the people were amazed, seeing him leaping and hearing his shouts of joy as he held fast to his two friends. A great throng gathered about them in the large open court, called Solomon's porch. Peter, seeing the throng, began to tell them about the wonderful Prince of Life, Jesus, whom they had put to death. Such preaching within the temple courts aroused the people and offended the priests, and the chief officer seized Peter and John and cast them into prison. This caused hundreds of the people to declare themselves Christians. The next morning when Peter and John were brought before the council and questioned, the officers said, "We will let you go if you will promise not to speak or teach in this name again." They answered: "Whether it be right in the sight of God to hearken unto you, rather than unto God, judge ye; for we cannot but speak the things we have seen and heard." So, failing to frighten them, the officers were compelled to let them go. And being let go, they returned at once to the Christian company, and told what the Jewish officers had said to them. And the cripple who had been healed at the Beautiful Gate was a happy Christian in that company, and was one of the loudest in giving true praise to God that day.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 一天下午，两个朋友走在耶路撒冷的街上，正赶往圣殿参加晚祭。在圣殿的一个入口——美门（因其雪白的大理石台阶通向那扇昂贵的黄铜大门而得名）——坐着一个可怜的瘸子在乞讨。他的双脚和脚踝残废，从来不能行走，甚至无法站立。他的朋友每天早上把这个无助的瘸子抬来，放在圣殿门口，向前来祷告的人乞讨施舍。那人一看见彼得和约翰这两个朋友，便可怜巴巴地喊道：“行行好，施舍点吧！”两人停下脚步，静静地注视着他的眼睛，说道：“看着我们！”他立刻满怀期待地抬起头来。彼得说：“金银我都没有，但我把我所有的给你。我奉拿撒勒人耶稣基督的名，叫你起来行走！”彼得拉着他的手扶他起来，他的双脚和踝骨立刻强健有力。那人跳了起来，同彼得、约翰一起走进圣殿，边走边跳，赞美神。众人见他又蹦又跳、紧紧拉着两位朋友欢呼，都惊讶不已。一大群人围聚到他们身边，挤满了那座名叫所罗门廊的宽阔庭院。彼得见人群聚拢，便开口向他们讲述奇妙的生命之君耶稣——正是被他们处死的那一位。这样在圣殿院内讲道，既鼓动了民众，又触怒了祭司，守殿官长便抓住彼得和约翰，把他们关进了监牢。即便如此，仍有数百人因此公开承认自己成了基督徒。第二天早晨，彼得和约翰被带到公会前受审，官长们说：“只要你们答应不再奉这名传讲、教导，我们就放你们走。”他们回答说：“听从你们而不听从神，这在神看来是否合宜，你们自己判断吧；我们所看见、所听见的，不能不说。”官长们既吓不倒他们，只得放了他们。一获释，他们立刻回到基督徒的团体中，把犹太官长对他们说的话讲了出来。那个在美门得了医治的瘸子，也是这群人中一位欢喜的基督徒，那天他赞美神的声音数一数二地响亮。

entries.title: The Ontario Readers: Third Book: THE HOME OF ROBIN IN SHERWOOD FOREST

paragraphs.text: Many hundreds of years ago, when the Plantagenets were kings, England was so covered with woods that a squirrel was said to be able to hop from tree to tree from the Severn to the Humber.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 许多个世纪以前，金雀花王朝当政的年代，英格兰林木遍布，据说一只松鼠能从塞文河一路在树间跳跃，直跳到亨伯河。

paragraphs.text: It must have been very different-looking from the country we travel through now; but still there were roads that ran from north to south and from east to west, for the use of those who wished to leave their homes, and at certain times of the year these roads were thronged with people. Pilgrims going to some holy shrine passed along, merchants taking their wares to Court, Abbots and Bishops ambling by on palfreys to bear their part in the King's Council, and, more frequently still, a solitary Knight, seeking adventures.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 那时的乡野，景象想必与我们如今穿行的大不相同；不过那时也已有南北纵横、东西交错的道路，供想要远行的人们使用，一年中的某些时节，这些路上挤满了行人。前往圣地朝拜的香客络绎而过，商人运着货物前往宫廷，修道院院长和主教骑着坐骑缓缓而行，赶去参与国王的议会，而更常见的，还是独自一人、四处寻觅冒险的骑士。

paragraphs.text: Besides the broad roads there were small tracks and little green paths, and these led to clumps of low huts, where dwelt the peasants, charcoal-burners, and plowmen, while here and there some larger clearing than usual told that the house of a yeoman was near.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 除了这些宽阔的大道，还有一条条小径和绿茵幽路，通向一丛丛低矮的茅舍，那里住着农夫、烧炭人和耕田的庄稼汉；而四处偶尔出现一片格外宽阔的空地，便说明附近住着一户自耕农。

paragraphs.text: Now and then as you passed through the forest you might ride by a splendid abbey, and catch a glimpse of monks in long black or white gowns, fishing in the streams and rivers that abound in this part of England, or casting nets in the fish ponds which were in the midst of the abbey gardens. Or you might chance to see a castle with round turrets and high battlements, circled by strong walls, and protected by a moat full of water.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 穿行林间，你时而会骑马经过一座宏伟的修道院，瞥见身披黑色或白色长袍的修士，在英格兰这一带遍布的溪流江河中垂钓，或在修道院花园中央的鱼塘里撒网。你也或许会碰巧望见一座城堡，圆形的塔楼、高耸的雉堞，四周环绕着坚固的城墙，还有一道注满水的护城河守护着。

paragraphs.text: This was the sort of England into which the famous Robin Hood was born. We know very little about him, who he was, or where he lived, except that for some reason he had offended the King, who had declared him an outlaw, so that any man might kill him and never pay a penalty for it.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 著名的罗宾汉，便诞生在这样一个英格兰。关于他，他究竟是谁、住在何处，我们所知甚少，只知道他不知为何得罪了国王，国王宣布他为亡命之徒，于是任何人都可以杀死他而不必承担任何罪责。

paragraphs.text: But, outlaw or not, the poor people loved him and looked on him as their friend, and many a stout fellow came to join him, and led a merry life in the greenwood, with moss and fern for bed, and for meat the King's deer, which it was death to slay.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 但不论是不是亡命之徒，穷苦百姓都爱戴他，把他当作自己的朋友，许多彪悍的好汉都来投奔他，在绿林中过着快活的日子，以苔藓和蕨草为床，以国王的鹿为食——而私猎国王的鹿本是死罪。

paragraphs.text: Peasants of all sorts, tillers of the land, yeomen, and, as some say, Knights, went on their ways freely, for of them Robin took no toll; but rich men with moneybags well filled trembled as they drew near to Sherwood Forest--who was to know whether behind every tree there did not lurk Robin Hood or some of his men?
paragraphs.translation_zh: 形形色色的农夫、耕地的庄稼人、自耕农，乃至有人所说的骑士，都能自由通行，因为罗宾从不向他们收取买路钱；可那些钱袋鼓鼓的富人，一走近舍伍德森林便心惊胆战——谁说得准每棵树后会不会就藏着罗宾汉或他的某个手下呢？

entries.title: The Prince: To the Magnificent Lorenzo Di Piero De' Medici

paragraphs.text: Those who strive to obtain the good graces of a prince are accustomed to come before him with such things as they hold most precious, or in which they see him take most delight; whence one often sees horses, arms, cloth of gold, precious stones, and similar ornaments presented to princes, worthy of their greatness.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 凡是想博取君主恩宠的人,惯常会带着自己最珍视的东西、或君主最为喜爱的东西,前去进献;因此人们常见到有人把骏马、兵器、金锦、宝石以及诸如此类与君主的尊贵相称的珍玩,献给君主。

paragraphs.text: Desiring therefore to present myself to your Magnificence with some testimony of my devotion towards you, I have not found among my possessions anything which I hold more dear than, or value so much as, the knowledge of the actions of great men, acquired by long experience in contemporary affairs, and a continual study of antiquity; which, having reflected upon it with great and prolonged diligence, I now send, digested into a little volume, to your Magnificence.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 因此,我也想献上某种东西,以表明我对殿下的一片忠忱;可遍寻我的所有,再没有什么比我对伟人事迹的认识更为珍贵、更值得我看重的了——这份认识,得自我在当代事务中的长久阅历和对古代历史的不断钻研。如今我把它反复深思、精心整理成这样一本小书,呈献给殿下。

paragraphs.text: And although I may consider this work unworthy of your countenance, nevertheless I trust much to your benignity that it may be acceptable, seeing that it is not possible for me to make a better gift than to offer you the opportunity of understanding in the shortest time all that I have learnt in so many years, and with so many troubles and dangers; which work I have not embellished with swelling or magnificent words, nor stuffed with rounded periods, nor with any extrinsic allurements or adornments whatever, with which so many are accustomed to embellish their works; for I have wished either that no honour should be given it, or else that the truth of the matter and the weightiness of the theme shall make it acceptable.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 尽管我自认这部作品也许配不上殿下的垂青,但我深信您宽厚仁慈,定会赏脸收纳;因为我实在拿不出比这更好的礼物了——它能让您在最短的时间里,领会我多年来历经无数艰辛与风险所学到的一切。这部作品,我既没有用浮夸华丽的辞藻去装点,也没有用铿锵的排比长句去堆砌,更没有用任何外在的浮华与卖弄去粉饰——许多人惯于用这些来装点自己的著作;我只盼着:它要么不必得到任何称誉,要么单凭内容的真实和主题的厚重,就能被接纳。

paragraphs.text: Nor do I hold with those who regard it as a presumption if a man of low and humble condition dare to discuss and settle the concerns of princes; because, just as those who draw landscapes place themselves below in the plain to contemplate the nature of the mountains and of lofty places, and in order to contemplate the plains place themselves upon high mountains, even so to understand the nature of the people it needs to be a prince, and to understand that of princes it needs to be of the people.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 有人认为,一个出身低微、地位卑下的人,竟敢议论并裁断君主的事务,未免僭越;对此我并不苟同。因为正如画风景的人,要观察山峦和高处的形势,便置身于低处的平原;要观察平原,便登上高高的山顶;同样,要透彻了解人民的本性,就得站在君主的位置上,而要透彻了解君主的本性,就得置身于人民之中。

paragraphs.text: Take then, your Magnificence, this little gift in the spirit in which I send it; wherein, if it be diligently read and considered by you, you will learn my extreme desire that you should attain that greatness which fortune and your other attributes promise. And if your Magnificence from the summit of your greatness will sometimes turn your eyes to these lower regions, you will see how unmeritedly I suffer a great and continued malignity of fortune.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 那么,殿下,请本着我进献时的这份心意,收下这份薄礼吧。倘若您肯用心研读、细加思量,就会明白我那殷切的期望:愿您终能成就那份命运与您自身禀赋所许诺的伟业。倘若殿下肯从您那崇高的顶峰,偶尔垂顾这低洼之地,您便会看到,我是何等无辜地承受着命运长久而深重的折磨。

entries.title: The Aesop for Children: THE FLIES AND THE HONEY

paragraphs.text: A jar of honey was upset and the sticky sweetness flowed out on the table. The sweet smell of the honey soon brought a large number of Flies buzzing around. They did not wait for an invitation. No, indeed; they settled right down, feet and all, to gorge themselves. The Flies were quickly smeared from head to foot with honey. Their wings stuck together. They could not pull their feet out of the sticky mass. And so they died, giving their lives for the sake of a taste of sweetness.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 一罐蜂蜜被打翻了，黏稠香甜的蜜汁淌了一桌子。蜜的甜香很快引来一大群苍蝇，嗡嗡地围着打转。它们才不会等谁来邀请呢；恰恰相反，它们立刻连脚带身子全扑了上去，狼吞虎咽起来。转眼间，苍蝇们就从头到脚沾满了蜂蜜，翅膀也粘在了一起，脚怎么也拔不出那黏糊糊的蜜浆。就这样，它们送了命——为贪图一点甜味而丢掉了性命。

entries.title: For the Children's Hour: 8. LET CHILDREN REPRODUCE YOUR STORIES

paragraphs.text: Children should be given an opportunity to tell and retell the stories heard. Children like to create, and whether it be with sand, wood, or words, the underlying processes are the same. For a child to retell a story means that he enters into the spirit of it, that he sees clearly the mental picture, that he feels the atmosphere and life of the story. In this way imagination, memory, language, and reason are enriched and, at the same time, the ethical principle of the story is more clearly impressed on the child's mind, to be assimilated at pleasure.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 应当给孩子机会，让他们讲述、复述听过的故事。孩子天生爱创造，无论用的是沙子、木头还是语言，背后的过程都是一样的。让孩子复述一个故事，意味着他要融入故事的情境，在脑海里清楚地看见那幅画面，体味故事的氛围与生命。这样一来，想象力、记忆力、语言和思辨力都得到了滋养；与此同时，故事所蕴含的道理也更清晰地印进孩子心里，任他自在地领会、吸收。

entries.title: The Ontario Readers: Third Book: A Narrow Escape

paragraphs.text: "At another time, when I was hunting in Ceylon, I was terrified to see a gigantic lion approaching, with the evident intention of devouring me. My gun was only loaded with bird-shot, and I had no other about me. The savage animal shook his head several times, uttered a loud roar, and prepared to spring. I turned to flee, and--my flesh creeps even now at the recollection of it--there, on the bank of a river that lay behind me, was a huge crocodile with his terrible jaws open ready to swallow me!
paragraphs.translation_zh: “还有一次，我在锡兰打猎，惊恐地看见一头巨狮逼近，分明是要把我吃掉。我枪里只装了打鸟的铅砂，身上再没带别的弹药。那头凶兽连连摇头，发出一声怒吼，准备纵身扑来。我转身就逃——如今回想起来仍叫我毛骨悚然——只见我身后的河岸上，竟趴着一条巨大的鳄鱼，张着可怕的血盆大口，正要把我吞下！

paragraphs.text: "Imagine, gentlemen, the horror of my situation--before me the lion, behind me the crocodile, on my left a rushing torrent, and on the right an abyss full of poisonous snakes! I gave myself up for lost, and fell to the ground in an almost fainting condition, expecting nothing better than to meet with a horrible death from one or the other of these terrible animals.
paragraphs.translation_zh: “先生们，请想象我处境之凶险——前有狮子，后有鳄鱼，左边是湍急的激流，右边是满是毒蛇的深渊！我自知必死无疑，几乎昏厥着瘫倒在地，只觉得横竖逃不出这几头恶兽的爪牙，唯有等死。

paragraphs.text: "After waiting a few seconds I heard a violent noise, different from any that had fallen on my ears before. I ventured to raise my head, and what do you think had happened?
paragraphs.translation_zh: “等了几秒钟，我听见一阵猛烈的声响，跟我以往听过的任何声音都不一样。我壮着胆子抬起头来，你猜发生了什么？

paragraphs.text: "The lion had, in his eagerness, jumped clean over me into the crocodile's jaws; the head of the one stuck in the throat of the other, and they were struggling to free themselves. I quickly sprang to my feet, drew out my hunting-knife, and with one blow severed the lion's head. Then, with the butt-end of my gun, I rammed the head farther into the throat of the crocodile, and destroyed him by suffocation. The hide of the crocodile, which was exactly forty feet in length, I had stuffed, and it now forms one of the chief attractions in the museum at Amsterdam, where the superintendent relates the story to all spectators, with harrowing additions.
paragraphs.translation_zh: “原来那头狮子情急之下，竟径直从我头顶越过，扑进了鳄鱼口中；狮头卡在鳄鱼的咽喉里，两头野兽都在拼命挣扎，想要脱身。我一跃而起，拔出猎刀，一刀砍下了狮子的头。然后用枪托把狮头往鳄鱼喉咙里捅得更深，活活将它闷死。那条鳄鱼的皮整整四十英尺长，我把它制成了标本，如今它成了阿姆斯特丹博物馆的一大看点；馆长向所有参观者讲述这段故事，还要添上些骇人的情节。

paragraphs.text: "One of these is that the lion jumped right through the crocodile, but as soon as the head appeared, Monsieur the Famous Baron (as he is pleased to call me) cut it off, and three feet of the crocodile's tail as well, whereupon the crocodile turned round, snatched the knife out of my hand, and swallowed it so greedily that it pierced his heart and killed him!
paragraphs.translation_zh: “其中一种说法是：狮子径直从鳄鱼身体里穿了过去，可狮头刚一冒出来，‘尊贵的著名男爵先生’（他爱这么称呼我）便一刀把它砍下，连鳄鱼三英尺长的尾巴也一并削掉；鳄鱼当即转身，从我手里夺过刀，狼吞虎咽地吞了下去，结果刀刺穿了它的心脏，把它送了命！

paragraphs.text: "I need not tell you how annoyed I was by these exaggerations. In this age of doubt people who do not know me might possibly be led to disbelieve the real facts when they are mixed up with such absurd inventions.
paragraphs.translation_zh: “不用我说，你们也知道这些夸大其词的说法让我多恼火。在这个事事生疑的年代，真相一旦掺进了这等荒唐的瞎编，不认识我的人难免会连真事也不肯相信了。

entries.title: The Children's Hour, Volume 3: THE BELLS]

paragraphs.text: The old mysteries, quaint plays in which long ago the peasants of Southern France acted the simple stories of the adoration of the Babe by the angels, the shepherds, and the Wise Men, are seen no more, but it is said that until very recently, in some of the provinces, at a certain pause in the mass, a shepherd knocks loudly on the great church door, the hollow sound echoing in the solemn hush. From without is heard singing, the voices of shepherds asking to come in. Slowly the doors swing back, the people part and the shepherds enter, passing up the nave between a double row of worshippers. In front are two or three boys playing softly on simple musical instruments, one has a flute, another a tambourine. Then begins a quaint musical dialogue between these peasants in their long, weather-stained cloaks, and those who stand on either side.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 那些古老的圣迹剧，那些古朴的戏文——很久以前法国南部的农人借它们演绎天使、牧羊人和东方贤士朝拜圣婴的质朴故事——如今已无处可见；但据说直到不久以前，在某些省份，弥撒进行到某个间歇时，一位牧羊人仍会重重叩响教堂的大门，空洞的声响在庄严的寂静中回荡。门外传来歌声，是牧羊人请求入内的呼唤。大门缓缓向内开启，众人让开一条路，牧羊人鱼贯而入，沿着中殿、从两列礼拜者之间走过。走在前头的是两三个男孩，轻轻奏着简单的乐器，一个吹长笛，一个敲手鼓。随后，这些身披沾满风霜的长斗篷的农人，便与两旁站立的人们，开始一场古雅的音乐对答。

paragraphs.text: So they move slowly, carrying a little fruit, a measure of grain, a pair of pigeons, to where the priest stands waiting to bless their simple gifts and lay them at the foot of the altar.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 于是他们慢慢地移动，带着一点水果、一点谷物、一对鸽子，来到牧师站着的地方，等待祝福他们简单的礼物，并将它们放在祭坛脚下。

entries.title: The Aesop for Children: THE WOLF AND THE LAMB

paragraphs.text: A stray Lamb stood drinking early one morning on the bank of a woodland stream. That very same morning a hungry Wolf came by farther up the stream, hunting for something to eat. He soon got his eyes on the Lamb. As a rule Mr. Wolf snapped up such delicious morsels without making any bones about it, but this Lamb looked so very helpless and innocent that the Wolf felt he ought to have some kind of an excuse for taking its life.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 一天清晨,一只迷途的小羊站在林边溪流的岸上喝水。也就在那天早晨,一只饿狼顺着溪流上游走来,四处找吃的。它很快就盯上了那只小羊。照例,狼先生遇上这样的美味,总是毫不含糊地一口叼走,可这只小羊看上去那么无助、那么无辜,狼竟觉得自己总该找个借口,才好下口取它的性命。

paragraphs.text: "How dare you paddle around in my stream and stir up all the mud!" he shouted fiercely. "You deserve to be punished severely for your rashness!"
paragraphs.translation_zh: “你好大的胆子,竟敢在我的溪水里乱踩,把泥浆全搅了起来!”它凶狠地吼道,“就凭你这般莽撞,也该重重地罚你一顿!”

paragraphs.text: "But, your highness," replied the trembling Lamb, "do not be angry! I cannot possibly muddy the water you are drinking up there. Remember, you are upstream and I am downstream."
paragraphs.translation_zh: “可是,殿下,”小羊浑身发抖地答道,“您别动怒!您在上头喝的水,我无论如何也弄不浑呀。您想想,您在上游,我在下游呢。”

paragraphs.text: "You _do_ muddy it!" retorted the Wolf savagely. "And besides, I have heard that you told lies about me last year!"
paragraphs.translation_zh: “你就是把水搅浑了!”狼凶狠地反驳道,“再说,我还听说你去年到处说我的坏话!”

paragraphs.text: "Well, then," snarled the Wolf, "It was someone in your family anyway. But no matter who it was, I do not intend to be talked out of my breakfast."
paragraphs.translation_zh: “那好吧,”狼咆哮道,“就算不是你,那也是你们家的哪一个干的。可不管是谁,我都不打算让你三言两语,就把我这顿早餐给说没了。”

entries.title: How to Tell Stories to Children: THE BUCKWHEAT

paragraphs.text: Down by the river were fields of barley and rye and golden oats. Wheat grew there, too, and the heaviest and richest ears bent lowest, in humility. Opposite the corn was a field of buckwheat, but the buckwheat never bent; it held its head proud and stiff on the stem.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 河边是大麦、黑麦和金黄燕麦的田地。那里也长着小麦，最沉甸、最饱满的麦穗谦卑地垂得最低。谷田对面是一片荞麦，可荞麦从不低头，它在茎秆上高傲而僵直地昂着脑袋。

paragraphs.text: One day a dreadful storm came. The field-flowers folded their leaves together, and bowed their heads. But the buckwheat stood straight and proud.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 有一天，一场可怕的暴风雨来了。田野里的花儿合拢叶子，低下了头。唯独荞麦依旧挺直腰杆，高傲不屈。

paragraphs.text: "Bend your head, as we do!" warned the golden wheat-ears; "the angel of the storm is coming; he will strike you down."
paragraphs.translation_zh: “快像我们一样低下头吧！”金色的麦穗劝告道，“风暴天使就要来了，他会把你打倒。”

paragraphs.text: Then the old willow-tree spoke: "Close your flowers and bend your leaves. Do not look at the lightning when the cloud bursts. Even men cannot do that; the sight of heaven would strike them blind. Much less can we who are so inferior to them!"
paragraphs.translation_zh: 接着，老柳树开口了：“合拢你的花，垂下你的叶。乌云迸裂时，千万别去看那闪电。连人都做不到——目睹那天威会让他们双目失明。我们这些远不如人类的植物，就更看不得了！”

paragraphs.text: "'Inferior,' indeed!" said the buckwheat. "Now I _will_ look!" And he looked straight up, while the lightning flashed across the sky.
paragraphs.translation_zh: “哼，还说什么‘不如人’！”荞麦说，“这下我偏要看个究竟！”说着它直直地仰起头，闪电正划过长空。

paragraphs.text: When the dreadful storm had passed, the flowers and the wheat raised their drooping heads, clean and refreshed in the pure, sweet air. The willow-tree shook the gentle drops from its leaves.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 可怕的暴风雨过去后，花儿和小麦都抬起低垂的头，在纯净甜美的空气里显得洁净而清新。柳树轻轻抖落叶上的水珠。

entries.title: The Aesop for Children: THE EAGLE AND THE BEETLE

paragraphs.text: A Beetle once begged the Eagle to spare a Hare which had run to her for protection. But the Eagle pounced upon her prey, the sweep of her great wings tumbling the Beetle a dozen feet away. Furious at the disrespect shown her, the Beetle flew to the Eagle's nest and rolled out the eggs. Not one did she spare. The Eagle's grief and anger knew no bounds, but who had done the cruel deed she did not know.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 有一次，一只甲虫恳求老鹰放过一只跑来向它求救的野兔。老鹰却一把扑向猎物，巨大的翅膀一扫，把甲虫掀出去十几英尺远。甲虫为自己受到的轻慢勃然大怒，飞到老鹰巢里，把蛋一个个滚了出去，一个也没留下。老鹰悲愤交加，却不知道这桩狠事是谁干的。

paragraphs.text: Next year the Eagle built her nest far up on a mountain crag; but the Beetle found it and again destroyed the eggs. In despair the Eagle now implored great Jupiter to let her place her eggs in his lap. There none would dare harm them. But the Beetle buzzed about Jupiter's head, and made him rise to drive her away; and the eggs rolled from his lap.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 第二年，老鹰把巢筑到了高高的山崖上；可甲虫还是找到了，又一次毁掉了鹰蛋。绝望之下，老鹰恳求伟大的朱庇特，让它把蛋产在他的膝上——那里谁也不敢加害。可甲虫绕着朱庇特的头嗡嗡乱飞，逼得他站起身去驱赶，蛋便从他膝上滚落了下来。

paragraphs.text: Now the Beetle told the reason for her action, and Jupiter had to acknowledge the justice of her cause. And they say that ever after, while the Eagle's eggs lie in the nest in spring, the Beetle still sleeps in the ground. For so Jupiter commanded.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 这时甲虫才说出自己这么做的缘由，朱庇特不得不承认它占着理。据说从那以后，每年春天老鹰的蛋躺在巢里时，甲虫总还睡在地下——因为这是朱庇特定下的规矩。

entries.title: The Yellow Fairy Book: THE COCK AND THE PEARL

paragraphs.text: A cock was once strutting up and down the farmyard among the hens when suddenly he espied something shining and the straw. "Ho! ho!" quoth he, "that's for me," and soon rooted it out from beneath the straw. What did it turn out to be but a Pearl that by some chance had been lost in the yard? "You may be a treasure," quoth Master Cock, "to men that prize you, but for me I would rather have a single barley corn than a peck of pearls."
paragraphs.translation_zh: 有一次，一只公鸡在农家院子里、母鸡群中昂首阔步地踱来踱去，忽然瞥见稻草里有个闪闪发光的东西。“嗬！嗬！”它说，“这才是给我的好东西。”很快便把它从稻草底下刨了出来。原来竟是一颗不知怎么丢在院子里的珍珠！“对珍视你的人来说，你或许是件宝贝，”公鸡先生说，“可对我来说，我宁要一粒大麦，也不要满满一斗珍珠。”

entries.title: The Aesop for Children: THE MISCHIEVOUS DOG

paragraphs.text: There was once a Dog who was so ill-natured and mischievous that his Master had to fasten a heavy wooden clog about his neck to keep him from annoying visitors and neighbors. But the Dog seemed to be very proud of the clog and dragged it about noisily as if he wished to attract everybody's attention. He was not able to impress anyone.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 从前有一只狗，脾气又坏又爱捣乱，主人不得不在它脖子上拴了一块沉重的木枷，免得它去骚扰客人和邻居。可这只狗似乎对那块木枷颇为得意，拖着它哗啦作响地四处招摇，仿佛想引起所有人的注意。然而它没能让任何人觉得它了不起。

paragraphs.text: "You would be wiser," said an old acquaintance, "to keep quietly out of sight with that clog. Do you want everybody to know what a disgraceful and ill-natured Dog you are?"
paragraphs.translation_zh: 一位老相识对它说：“你还是放聪明些，带着那块木枷悄悄躲起来别露面才好。难道你想让所有人都知道，你是一只多么丢脸、多么坏脾气的狗吗？”

entries.title: For the Children's Hour: 13. THE EMPEROR AND THE BIRD'S NEST

paragraphs.text: "Look!" said a soldier; "look! a swallow has built her nest in the emperor's tent." The soldiers looked and saw a swallow's nest built of clay and horse's hair, and the swallow sitting on her eggs.
paragraphs.translation_zh: “看！”一名士兵说道；“快看，一只燕子在皇帐里筑巢了。”士兵们一看，只见一个用泥土和马毛筑成的燕子窝，燕子正坐在蛋上。

paragraphs.text: "Sure, the swallow thinks the emperor's tent is a shed," laughed the soldiers. The emperor, hearing his name spoken, came out from his tent. When he saw the nest, he said, "Let no hand molest the nest or hurt the bird."
paragraphs.translation_zh: “可不是嘛,燕子准把皇帝的营帐当成草棚啦。”士兵们笑道。皇帝听到有人提起自己,便从帐中走了出来。一看见那鸟巢,他就吩咐道:“谁也不许碰这巢,不许伤害这只鸟。”

paragraphs.text: So the little swallow sat there quietly, amid all the noise of cannon, hatching out her little ones, until at last the great guns had made a breach through the walls and the army had poured in to take the city. Then when the terrible fighting was ended, the soldiers began taking down their tents to go away; but when they came to the emperor's tent, he said, "No, no! do not take down my tent, leave it standing!"
paragraphs.translation_zh: 于是小燕子静静地伏在那里,在隆隆炮火声中孵着她的雏鸟,直到大炮终于在城墙上轰开缺口,大军蜂拥而入,攻占了这座城。等那场惨烈的战斗结束,士兵们开始拆帐篷准备撤离;可一走到皇帝的营帐前,他却说:“不,不!别拆我的帐篷,让它留在那儿!”

paragraphs.text: So it stood there all alone, Loosely flapping, torn and tattered, Till the brood was fledged and flown, Singing o'er those walls of stone Which the cannon-shot had shattered.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 于是它孤零零地立在那里,松垮垮地飘动着,残破而褴褛,直到一窝雏燕羽翼丰满、振翅飞走,在那被炮弹轰碎的石墙上空一路欢唱。

entries.title: The Aesop for Children: THE NORTH WIND AND THE SUN

paragraphs.text: The North Wind and the Sun had a quarrel about which of them was the stronger. While they were disputing with much heat and bluster, a Traveler passed along the road wrapped in a cloak.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 北风和太阳争论谁更强。就在他们激烈争论的时候，一个穿着斗篷的旅行者从路上走过。

paragraphs.text: "Let us agree," said the Sun, "that he is the stronger who can strip that Traveler of his cloak."
paragraphs.translation_zh: “咱们说定，”太阳说，“谁能让那位旅行者脱下斗篷，谁就更强。”

paragraphs.text: With the first gust of wind the ends of the cloak whipped about the Traveler's body. But he immediately wrapped it closely around him, and the harder the Wind blew, the tighter he held it to him. The North Wind tore angrily at the cloak, but all his efforts were in vain.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 第一阵风刮来，斗篷的下摆便抽打着旅行者的身体。但他立刻把斗篷紧紧裹住，风刮得越猛，他就裹得越紧。北风愤怒地撕扯斗篷，一切努力却都是徒劳。

paragraphs.text: Then the Sun began to shine. At first his beams were gentle, and in the pleasant warmth after the bitter cold of the North Wind, the Traveler unfastened his cloak and let it hang loosely from his shoulders. The Sun's rays grew warmer and warmer. The man took off his cap and mopped his brow. At last he became so heated that he pulled off his cloak, and, to escape the blazing sunshine, threw himself down in the welcome shade of a tree by the roadside.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 接着，太阳放出光来。起初阳光柔和，捱过北风的刺骨严寒之后，这暖意叫人舒坦，旅行者便解开斗篷，任它松松地搭在肩头。阳光越来越暖。旅人摘下帽子，擦了擦额头。最后他热得受不住，索性脱下斗篷，为了躲开炎炎烈日，一头扑进了路边一棵树那令人惬意的浓荫里。

entries.title: The Ontario Readers: The High School Reader: THE STORY OF A FIRE

paragraphs.text: Thirteen years have passed since, but it is all to me as if it had happened yesterday,--the clanging of the fire-bells, the hoarse shouts of the firemen, the wild rush and terror of the streets; then the great hush that fell upon the crowd; the sea of upturned faces with the fire glow upon it; and up there, against the background of black smoke that poured from roof to attic, the boy clinging to the narrow ledge, so far up that it seemed humanly impossible that help could ever come.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 十三年过去了，可在我心里，那一切都仿佛发生在昨天——火警钟当当作响，消防员声嘶力竭地呼喊，街上人群疯狂奔逃、一片惊恐；接着，一阵巨大的寂静落在人群头上；一张张仰起的脸汇成人海，火光在上面跳动；而在那高处，在浓烟从屋顶直涌向阁楼的背景前，一个男孩紧攀在窄窄的窗台上，那么高，高得仿佛人力绝无可能施救。

paragraphs.text: But even then it was coming. Up from the street, while the crew of the truck company were labouring with the heavy extension ladder that at its longest stretch was many feet too short, crept four men upon long, slender poles with cross-bars, iron-hooked at the end. Standing in one window, they reached up and thrust the hook through the next one above, then mounted a story higher. Again the crash of glass, and again the dizzy ascent. Straight up the wall they crept, looking like human flies on the ceiling, and clinging as close, never resting, reaching one recess only to set out for the next; nearer and nearer in the race for life, until but a single span separated the foremost from the boy. And now the iron hook fell at his feet, and the fireman stood upon the step with the rescued lad in his arms, just as the pent-up flames burst lurid from the attic window, reaching with impotent fury for their prey. The next moment they were safe upon the great ladder waiting to receive them below.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 可就在此时，救援已经赶来。云梯队的队员们在街面上吃力地操弄着那架沉重的伸缩云梯——它就算伸到最长，也还差着好几英尺；这时，四个人攀着细长的杆梯往上爬，杆上带着横档，末端装着铁钩。他们站在一扇窗口，向上把钩子探进上面那扇窗，再爬高一层。又是一阵玻璃碎裂声，又是一段令人头晕的攀升。他们径直顺着墙往上爬，活像天花板上的苍蝇，紧紧贴着墙面，片刻不歇，刚够到一处窗洞，又向下一处进发；在这场与死神赛跑中越来越近，直到最前头那人离男孩只剩一臂之遥。这时铁钩搭到了男孩脚边，消防员怀里抱着获救的孩子，稳稳站上了横档，恰在此刻，憋积的火焰从阁楼窗口骇人地窜出，徒劳地张着狂怒的爪子扑向猎物。再下一刻，他们已安全地踏上了那架在下方等候接应的大云梯。

paragraphs.text: Then such a shout went up! Men fell on each other's necks, and cried and laughed at once. Strangers slapped one another on the back with glistening faces, shook hands, and behaved generally like men gone suddenly mad. Women wept in the street. The driver of a car stalled in the crowd, who had stood through it all speechless, clutching the reins, whipped his horses into a gallop and drove away, yelling like a Comanche, to relieve his feelings. The boy and his rescuer were carried across the street without anyone knowing how. Policemen forgot their dignity and shouted with the rest. Fire, peril, terror, and loss were alike forgotten in the one touch of nature that makes the whole world kin.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 顿时爆发出一片欢呼！人们互相搂抱，又哭又笑。素不相识的人彼此拍着后背，个个满脸泪光，握手言欢，简直像一下子都疯了。妇女们在街上落泪。一辆马车堵在人群里，车夫自始至终一言不发地攥着缰绳，这时猛地扬鞭，策马飞奔而去，像科曼奇人一样高声呼喊，好发泄心头的激动。男孩和救他的人被人们抬过街去，谁也说不清是怎么过去的。警察也忘了自己的威严，跟着众人一起欢呼。火灾、危险、惊恐与损失，全都被一同抛在脑后——正是这一点共通的人性，让全世界亲如一家。

paragraphs.text: Fireman John Binns was made captain of his crew, and the Bennett medal was pinned on his coat on the next parade day.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 消防员约翰·宾斯被提升为他那一队的队长，在随后的阅兵日，贝内特奖章别上了他的外衣。

entries.title: McGuffey's Sixth Eclectic Reader: IV. INSTRUCTIONS FOR READING VERSE. (39)

paragraphs.text: In reading verse, the inflections should be nearly the same as in reading prose; the chief difference is, that in poetry, the monotone and rising inflection are more frequently used than in prose. The greatest difficulty in reading this species of composition, consists in giving it that measured flow which distinguishes it from prose, without falling into a chanting pronunciation.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 朗读诗歌时，语调变化应当和朗读散文基本相同；主要区别在于，诗歌比散文更常使用平调和升调。朗读这类作品最难的地方，是读出它区别于散文的节奏感，同时又不落入吟唱式的腔调。

paragraphs.text: If, at any time, the reader is in doubt as to the proper inflection, let him reduce the passage to earnest conversation, and pronounce it in the most familiar and prosaic manner, and thus he will generally use the proper inflection.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 如果读者一时拿不准该用什么语调，就把那段文字当作认真交谈的话来处理，用最自然、最像散文的方式读出来，这样通常就能用对语调。

entries.title: The Aesop for Children: THE WOLF AND THE GOAT

paragraphs.text: A hungry Wolf spied a Goat browsing at the top of a steep cliff where he could not possibly get at her.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 一只饥饿的狼看见一只山羊正在陡峭的悬崖顶上吃草，他根本不可能够到她。

paragraphs.text: "That is a very dangerous place for you," he called out, pretending to be very anxious about the Goat's safety. "What if you should fall! Please listen to me and come down! Here you can get all you want of the finest, tenderest grass in the country."
paragraphs.translation_zh: “你待的地方太危险了，”他喊道，装出一副非常担心山羊安全的样子，“万一你掉下来怎么办！请听我的，快下来吧！这下面有全国最鲜嫩的青草，想吃多少都有。”

paragraphs.text: "How very, very anxious you are about me," she said, "and how generous you are with your grass! But I know you! It's your _own_ appetite you are thinking of, not mine!"
paragraphs.translation_zh: “你可真是太替我操心了，”山羊说，“还这么大方，要把你的草分给我！不过我了解你！你惦记的不是我的胃口，而是你自己的胃口！”

paragraphs.text: Though coffee has become a global commodity, it has a history tied to food traditions around the Red Sea. The earliest reports of coffee drinking pertain to the plant's use among the Sufis of Yemen in southern Arabia in the middle of the 15th century. Up to the end of the 17th century, most of the world's coffee was imported from Yemen. As it gained in popularity, coffee started to be cultivated in Java in the 17th century and the Americas from the 18th century.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 尽管咖啡已成为一种全球性商品，但其历史与红海周边地区的饮食传统紧密相连。关于饮用咖啡的最早记载，与15世纪中叶阿拉伯半岛南部也门的苏菲派对这种植物的使用有关。直到17世纪末，世界上大部分咖啡都从也门进口。随着咖啡日益普及，17世纪起咖啡开始在爪哇种植，18世纪起又在美洲种植。

paragraphs.text: Track bicycles do not have brakes, because all riders ride in the same direction around a track which does not necessitate sharp deceleration. Track riders are still able to slow down because all track bicycles are fixed-gear, meaning that there is no freewheel. Without a freewheel, coasting is impossible, so when the rear wheel is moving, the cranks are moving. To slow down, the rider applies resistance to the pedals, acting as a braking system which can be as effective as a conventional rear wheel brake, but not as effective as a front wheel brake.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 场地自行车没有刹车，因为所有骑手都沿同一方向绕赛道骑行，无需急剧减速。场地骑手仍然能够减速，因为所有场地自行车都是固定齿轮的，也就是说没有飞轮。没有飞轮就无法滑行，因此只要后轮在转动，曲柄也在转动。为了减速，骑手会对踏板施加阻力，这相当于一套制动系统，其效果可与传统的后轮刹车相当，但不及前轮刹车。

paragraphs.text: Flour is grain ground into a powder. Flour provides the primary structure, starch and protein to the final baked bread. The protein content of the flour is the best indicator of the quality of the bread dough and the finished bread. While bread can be made from all-purpose wheat flour, a specialty bread flour, containing more protein, is recommended for high-quality bread. If one uses a flour with a lower protein content to produce bread, a shorter mixing time is required to develop gluten strength properly.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 面粉是磨成粉末的谷物。面粉为最终烤制的面包提供主要的结构、淀粉和蛋白质。面粉的蛋白质含量是衡量面团和成品面包质量的最佳指标。虽然面包可以用通用小麦面粉制作，但要做出高品质的面包，建议使用蛋白质含量更高的专用面包粉。如果用蛋白质含量较低的面粉制作面包，则需要较短的搅拌时间才能让面筋强度得到适当发展。

paragraphs.text: Airport operations are extremely complex, with a complicated system of aircraft support services, passenger services, and aircraft control services contained within the operation. Thus airports can be major employers, as well as important hubs for tourism and other kinds of transit. Because they are sites of operation for heavy machinery, a number of regulations and safety measures have been implemented in airports, in order to reduce hazards. Additionally, airports have major local environmental impacts, as both large sources of air pollution, noise pollution and other environmental impacts, making them sites that acutely experience the environmental effects of aviation.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 机场运营极为复杂，其运作中包含一套由飞机保障服务、旅客服务和飞机管制服务构成的复杂系统。因此，机场可以成为重要的雇主，也是旅游和其他各类中转的重要枢纽。由于机场是重型机械的作业场所，为减少危险，机场内实施了许多规章制度和安全措施。此外，机场对当地环境有重大影响，既是空气污染、噪声污染的大型来源，也带来其他环境影响，使其成为强烈承受航空业环境后果的场所。

paragraphs.text: Coffee appears to have been likely collected from wild, with some indications that its use expanded from the 14th century among certain Islamized groups in southeastern Ethiopia, though direct evidence for early consumption remains scarce. The use of coffee is believed to have spread across the Red Sea to the Rasulid sultanate of Yemen, who maintained cultural and commercial ties with the Adal Sultanate. Its consumption first appears in Yemen, particularly in regions such as Aden, Mocha and Zabid during the 15th century. The 16th-century scholar Ibn Hajar al-Haytami writes about the plant's development from a tree in the Zeila region. In 1542, a Portuguese crew met with a ship from Zeila transporting clarified butter and coffee to Al-Shihr in Yemen.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 咖啡最初很可能采集自野生植株,有迹象表明,从14世纪起,咖啡的使用在埃塞俄比亚东南部某些已伊斯兰化的族群中逐渐扩展,不过关于早期饮用咖啡的直接证据仍然稀少。人们相信,饮用咖啡的习惯越过红海传播到了也门的拉苏里德苏丹国,该苏丹国与阿达尔苏丹国保持着文化和商业往来。咖啡的饮用最早出现在也门,15世纪时尤其集中于亚丁、摩卡和宰比德等地区。16世纪学者伊本·哈杰尔·海塔米记述了这种植物在泽拉地区由一种树木发展而来的过程。1542年,一支葡萄牙船队遇到了一艘来自泽拉的船只,这艘船正把酥油和咖啡运往也门的希赫尔。

paragraphs.text: Storage conditions determine the shelf life of tea; black tea shelf life is greater than that of green teas. Some, such as flower teas, may last only a month or so. Others, such as pu-erh, improve with age. To remain fresh and prevent mould, tea needs to be stored away from heat, light, air, and moisture. Tea must be kept at room temperature in an air-tight container. Black tea in a bag within a sealed opaque canister may keep for two years.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 储存条件决定着茶叶的保质期;红茶的保质期比绿茶更长。有些茶,比如花茶,可能只能存放一个月左右。另一些茶,比如普洱,则会随着存放时间越久而品质越好。为了保持新鲜并防止发霉,茶叶需要存放在远离高温、光照、空气和潮湿的环境中。茶叶必须在室温下存放于密封容器中。用茶袋装好、再放入密封不透光罐中的红茶,可以保存两年。

paragraphs.text: The most common form today is a residential or public garden, but the term garden has traditionally been a more general one. Zoos, which display wild animals in simulated natural habitats, were formerly called zoological gardens. Western gardens are almost universally based on plants, with garden, which etymologically implies enclosure, often signifying a shortened form of botanical garden. Some traditional types of eastern gardens, such as Zen gardens, however, use plants sparsely or not at all. Landscape gardens, on the other hand, such as the English landscape gardens first developed in the 18th century, may decide to omit flowers altogether.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 如今最常见的花园形式是住宅花园或公共花园,但花园一词在传统上含义要宽泛得多。动物园在模拟的自然栖息地中展示野生动物,过去曾被称为动物园林。西方花园几乎无一例外都以植物为基础,花园一词在词源上含有围合之地的意思,通常是植物园的简略说法。然而,某些传统的东方园林,例如禅意庭园,很少使用植物,甚至完全不用。另一方面,景观式园林,例如最早在18世纪发展起来的英式景观园林,可能会完全不种花卉。

paragraphs.text: Airport operations are extremely complex, with a complicated system of aircraft support services, passenger services, and aircraft control services contained within the operation. Thus airports can be major employers, as well as important hubs for tourism and other kinds of transit. Because they are sites of operation for heavy machinery, a number of regulations and safety measures have been implemented in airports, in order to reduce hazards. Additionally, airports have major local environmental impacts, as both large sources of air pollution, noise pollution and other environmental impacts, making them sites that acutely experience the environmental effects of aviation. Airports are also vulnerable infrastructure to extreme weather, climate change caused sea level rise and other disasters.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 机场的运营极其复杂,其运作中包含着由飞机保障服务、旅客服务和飞机管制服务构成的复杂系统。因此,机场既可以是重要的用人单位,也是旅游和其他各类中转的重要枢纽。由于机场是重型机械的作业场所,为了降低危险,机场已实施了一系列规章制度和安全措施。此外,机场对当地环境有重大影响,既是空气污染、噪声污染和其他环境影响的主要来源,也使其成为最直接承受航空业环境后果的场所。机场也是易受极端天气、气候变化导致的海平面上升以及其他灾害影响的脆弱基础设施。

paragraphs.text: Board of Trustees or Board of directors, The board governs the museum and is responsible for ensuring the museum is financially and ethically sound. They set standards and policies for the museum. Board members are often involved in fundraising aspects of the museum and represent the institution. Some museums use the terms "directors" and "trustees" interchangeably but both are different legal instruments. A board of directors governs a nonprofit corporation, a board of trustees is responsible for governing a charitable, educational, scientific, or religious trust, foundation, or endowment.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 博物馆的理事会或董事会负责治理博物馆,并有责任确保博物馆在财务和道德方面都健全可靠。他们为博物馆制定标准和政策。理事会成员通常会参与博物馆筹款方面的工作,并代表该机构。有些博物馆把董事和理事这两个称谓混用,但二者在法律上其实是不同的概念。董事会治理的是非营利法人,而理事会则负责治理慈善、教育、科学或宗教性质的信托、基金会或捐赠基金。

paragraphs.text: Storage conditions determine the shelf life of tea; black tea shelf life is greater than that of green teas. Some, such as flower teas, may last only a month or so. Others, such as pu-erh, improve with age. To remain fresh and prevent mould, tea needs to be stored away from heat, light, air, and moisture. Tea must be kept at room temperature in an air-tight container. Black tea in a bag within a sealed opaque canister may keep for two years. Green tea deteriorates more rapidly, usually in less than a year.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 储存条件决定茶叶的保质期;红茶的保质期比绿茶更长。有些茶,例如花茶,可能只能存放一个月左右。另一些茶,例如普洱,则会随着存放时间增长而品质提升。为保持新鲜并防止发霉,茶叶需要储存在远离高温、光照、空气和湿气的地方。茶叶必须在室温下保存于密封容器中。装入袋中、再放进密封不透光罐子里的红茶,可以保存两年。绿茶变质更快,通常不到一年就会变质。

paragraphs.text: The Chorleywood bread process was developed in 1961; it uses the intense mechanical working of dough to dramatically reduce the fermentation period and the time taken to produce a loaf. The process, whose high-energy mixing allows for the use of grain with a lower protein content, is now widely used around the world in large factories. As a result, bread can be produced very quickly and at low costs to the manufacturer and the consumer. However, there has been some criticism of the effect on nutritional value.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 乔利伍德面包制作法于1961年问世，它通过对面团进行剧烈的机械搅拌，大幅缩短了发酵时间和制作一条面包所需的总时长。这种工艺的高能搅拌使得低蛋白含量的谷物也能用于制作面包，如今已在世界各地的大型工厂中广泛采用。因此，面包得以快速生产，生产商和消费者的成本也都很低。不过，这种工艺对面包营养价值的影响也招致了一些批评。

paragraphs.text: Tourists around the world can enjoy dining services on railway dining cars and cruise ship dining rooms, in some cities a restaurant tram is operated, which are essentially travelling restaurants. Most cruise ships provide a variety of dining experiences including a main restaurant, satellite restaurants, room service, speciality restaurants, cafes, bars and buffets to name a few. Some restaurants on these cruise ships require table reservations and operate specific dress codes. The railway dining services also cater to the needs of travellers by providing railway refreshment rooms at railway stations.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 世界各地的游客可以在铁路餐车和游轮餐厅中享用餐饮服务，一些城市还运营着餐厅有轨电车，这些本质上都是流动的餐厅。大多数游轮提供多种多样的用餐体验，例如主餐厅、附属餐厅、客房送餐、特色餐厅、咖啡馆、酒吧和自助餐等等。这些游轮上的部分餐厅需要预订餐位，并执行特定的着装要求。铁路餐饮服务还在火车站设置餐饮室，以满足旅客的需要。

paragraphs.text: Only the top 25 to 50 millimetres of the mature plant are picked. These buds and leaves are called 'flushes'. A plant will grow a new flush every 7 to 15 days during the growing season. Leaves that are slow in development tend to produce better-flavoured teas. Several teas are available from specified flushes; for example, Darjeeling tea is available as first flush, second flush, monsoon and autumn. Assam second flush or "tippy" tea is considered superior to first flush, because of the gold tips that appear on the leaves.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 只采摘成熟茶树顶部25至50毫米的部分。这些嫩芽和嫩叶被称为“新梢”。在生长季节，茶树每7到15天就会长出一轮新梢。生长缓慢的叶片往往能制出风味更佳的茶。有几种茶按特定采摘轮次区分；例如，大吉岭茶就分为头采、二采、雨季采和秋采。阿萨姆二采茶（即“金毫”茶）被认为优于头采茶，因为叶片上会出现金色的芽尖。

paragraphs.text: Early eating establishments recognizable as restaurants in the modern sense emerged in Song dynasty China during the 11th and 12th centuries. In large cities, such as Kaifeng and Hangzhou, food catering establishments catered to merchants who travelled between cities. Probably growing out of tea houses and taverns which catered to travellers, Kaifeng's restaurants blossomed into an industry that catered to locals as well as people from other regions of China. As travelling merchants were not used to the local cuisine of other cities, these establishments were set up to serve dishes familiar to merchants from other parts of China. Such establishments were located in the entertainment districts of major cities, alongside hotels, bars, and brothels.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 在11世纪和12世纪的中国宋朝，出现了可被视为现代意义上餐馆的早期餐饮场所。在开封、杭州等大城市，餐饮场所为往来于各城之间的商人提供服务。开封的餐馆大概是从招待旅客的茶馆和酒肆发展而来的，后来兴盛为一个既服务本地人、也服务来自中国其他地区之人的行业。由于行商不习惯其他城市的本地菜肴，这些场所便专门供应来自中国各地的商人所熟悉的菜品。这类场所坐落于大城市的娱乐区，与旅馆、酒馆和妓院比邻。

paragraphs.text: Bacteria are ubiquitous, mostly free-living organisms often consisting of one biological cell. They constitute a large domain of prokaryotic microorganisms. Typically a few micrometres in length, bacteria were among the first life forms to appear on Earth, and are present in most of its habitats. Bacteria inhabit the air, soil, water, acidic hot springs, radioactive waste, and the deep biosphere of Earth's crust. Bacteria play a vital role in many stages of the nutrient cycle by recycling nutrients and the fixation of nitrogen from the atmosphere.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 细菌无处不在，大多为自由生活的生物，通常由一个生物细胞构成。细菌构成了原核微生物中的一个庞大域。细菌通常只有几微米长，是地球上最早出现的生命形式之一，存在于地球的大多数生境中。细菌栖息于空气、土壤、水、酸性温泉、放射性废料以及地壳深部的生物圈中。细菌通过循环利用养分以及固定大气中的氮，在养分循环的许多环节中发挥着至关重要的作用。

paragraphs.text: Some components, which are often optional accessories on sports bicycles, are standard features on utility bicycles to enhance their usefulness, comfort, safety and visibility. Fenders with spoilers protect the cyclist and moving parts from spray when riding through wet areas. In some countries, fenders are called mudguards. The chainguards protect clothes from oil on the chain while preventing clothing from being caught between the chain and crankset teeth. Kick stands keep bicycles upright when parked, and bike locks deter theft.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 有些部件在运动自行车上往往是可选配件,而在实用型自行车上则是标准配置,用以提升其实用性、舒适性、安全性和可见性。带挡水板的挡泥板可以在骑行经过潮湿路段时,保护骑车人和运动部件免受水花飞溅。在一些国家,这种挡泥板有不同的称呼。链罩可以防止衣物沾上链条上的油污,同时避免衣物被卷入链条与牙盘齿之间。脚撑可以在停放时让自行车保持直立,车锁则能防止被盗。

paragraphs.text: Vaccine hesitancy is a delay in acceptance, or refusal of vaccines despite the availability of vaccine services. The term covers outright refusals to vaccinate, delaying vaccines, accepting vaccines but remaining uncertain about their use, or using certain vaccines but not others. There is an overwhelming scientific consensus that vaccines are generally safe and effective. Vaccine hesitancy often results in disease outbreaks and deaths from vaccine-preventable diseases. The World Health Organization therefore characterized vaccine hesitancy as one of the top ten global health threats in 2019.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 疫苗犹豫指的是在能够获得疫苗接种服务的情况下,仍推迟接种或拒绝接种疫苗。这一说法涵盖了彻底拒绝接种、推迟接种、接受接种但对其作用仍存疑虑,以及只接种某些疫苗而不接种其他疫苗等情形。科学界有压倒性的共识,认为疫苗总体上安全且有效。疫苗犹豫常常导致疾病暴发,并造成本可通过疫苗预防的疾病所引起的死亡。因此,世界卫生组织在2019年将疫苗犹豫列为全球十大健康威胁之一。

paragraphs.text: Bacteria are ubiquitous, mostly free-living organisms often consisting of one biological cell. They constitute a large domain of prokaryotic microorganisms. Typically a few micrometres in length, bacteria were among the first life forms to appear on Earth, and are present in most of its habitats. Bacteria inhabit the air, soil, water, acidic hot springs, radioactive waste, and the deep biosphere of Earth's crust. Bacteria play a vital role in many stages of the nutrient cycle by recycling nutrients and the fixation of nitrogen from the atmosphere. The nutrient cycle includes the decomposition of dead bodies; bacteria are responsible for the putrefaction stage in this process.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 细菌无处不在,大多营自由生活,通常由一个生物细胞构成。细菌构成了原核微生物中的一个庞大的域。细菌的长度通常只有几微米,是地球上最早出现的生命形式之一,存在于地球上大多数栖息环境中。细菌栖居在空气、土壤、水体、酸性温泉、放射性废物以及地壳深层的生物圈中。细菌通过循环利用养分以及固定大气中的氮,在养分循环的许多环节中发挥着至关重要的作用。养分循环包括对尸体的分解;在这一过程中,细菌负责其中的腐败阶段。

paragraphs.text: Toe-clips, toestraps and clipless pedals help keep the foot locked in the proper pedal position and enable cyclists to pull and push the pedals. Technical accessories include cyclocomputers for measuring speed, distance, heart rate, GPS data etc. Other accessories include lights, reflectors, mirrors, racks, trailers, bags, water bottles and cages, and bells. Bicycle lights, reflectors, and helmets are required by law in some geographic regions. It is more common to see bicycles with bottle generators, dynamos, lights, fenders, racks and bells in Europe.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 脚趾夹、脚趾带和自锁式踏板有助于把脚固定在正确的踏板位置,使骑行者既能向上拉也能向下蹬踏板。技术类配件包括用于测量速度、距离、心率和卫星定位数据等的码表。其他配件包括车灯、反光板、后视镜、货架、拖车、车包、水壶和水壶架,以及车铃。在某些地区,法律规定自行车必须配备车灯、反光板和头盔。在欧洲,更常见到配有瓶式发电机、花鼓发电机、车灯、挡泥板、货架和车铃的自行车。

paragraphs.text: Domestic cats use many vocalizations for communication, including purring, trilling, hissing, growling/snarling, grunting, and several different forms of meowing. Their body language, including position of ears and tail, relaxation of the whole body, and kneading of the paws, are all indicators of mood. The tail and ears are particularly important social signal mechanisms; a raised tail indicates a friendly greeting, and flattened ears indicate hostility. Tail-raising also indicates the cat's position in the group's social hierarchy, with dominant individuals raising their tails less often than subordinate ones. Feral cats are generally silent. Nose-to-nose touching is also a common greeting and may be followed by social grooming, which is solicited by one of the cats raising and tilting its head.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 家猫通过多种叫声进行交流,包括呼噜声、颤音、嘶嘶声、低吼或咆哮声、哼声,以及几种不同形式的喵叫声。猫的肢体语言,包括耳朵和尾巴的姿势、全身的放松程度,以及用爪子揉按的动作,都是其情绪的指示信号。尾巴和耳朵是尤为重要的社交信号机制;尾巴竖起表示友好的问候,耳朵贴平则表示敌意。竖尾还能体现猫在群体社会等级中的地位,地位高的个体竖尾的次数比地位低的个体更少。野猫通常不发出叫声。鼻子对鼻子的触碰也是一种常见的问候方式,之后可能会进行社交性的相互梳理,其中一只猫会抬起并侧偏头部来发出梳理的邀请。

paragraphs.text: Ground control is responsible for directing all ground traffic in designated "movement areas", except the traffic on runways. This includes planes, baggage trains, snowplows, grass cutters, fuel trucks, stair trucks, airline food trucks, conveyor belt vehicles and other vehicles. Ground Control will instruct these vehicles on which taxiways to use, which runway they will use, where they will park, and when it is safe to cross runways. When a plane is ready to takeoff it will be turned over to tower control. Conversely, after a plane has landed it will depart the runway and be "handed over" from Tower to Ground Control.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 地面管制负责指挥所有指定"活动区"内的地面交通,跑道上的交通除外。这包括飞机、行李拖车、扫雪车、割草机、加油车、客梯车、航空配餐车、传送带车以及其他车辆。地面管制会指示这些车辆使用哪些滑行道、使用哪条跑道、在何处停放,以及何时可以安全穿越跑道。当一架飞机准备起飞时,它会被移交给塔台管制。反之,飞机降落后会驶离跑道,并从塔台"移交"给地面管制。

paragraphs.text: Occasionally, postal authorities issue novelty "scented" or "aromatic" stamps which contain a scent, more readily apparent when rubbed. The effect is achieved by using ink which contains microcapsules that provide the desired fragrance when broken. The scent usually only lasts for a limited time after production, such as a few months or years. Such stamps are usually related to aromatic subjects including coffee, roses, grapes, chocolate, vanilla, cinnamon, pine needles or freshly baked bread. The first scented stamps were issued by Bhutan in 1973.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 邮政部门偶尔会发行新奇的“香味”邮票，这种邮票含有香气，经摩擦后香味会更明显。这种效果是通过使用含有微胶囊的油墨实现的，微胶囊破裂时便会散发出所需的香味。香味通常在生产后只能维持有限的一段时间，例如数月或数年。这类邮票的主题通常与带有香气的事物有关，包括咖啡、玫瑰、葡萄、巧克力、香草、肉桂、松针或刚出炉的面包。最早的香味邮票由不丹于1973年发行。

paragraphs.text: A study by the University of Hohenheim found that industrially produced bread typically has a high proportion of FODMAP carbohydrates due to a short rising time. The high proportion of FODMAP carbohydrates in such bread then causes flatulence. This is particularly problematic in intestinal diseases such as irritable bowel syndrome. While in traditional bread making the dough rises for several hours, industrial breads rise for a much shorter time, usually only one hour. However, a sufficiently long rising time is important to break down the indigestible FODMAP carbohydrates.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 霍恩海姆大学的一项研究发现，工业化生产的面包由于发酵时间短，通常含有较高比例的可发酵碳水化合物。这类面包中高比例的可发酵碳水化合物随后会引起胀气。对于肠易激综合征等肠道疾病患者来说，这尤其成问题。在传统面包制作中，面团要发酵数小时，而工业面包的发酵时间则短得多，通常只有一小时。然而，足够长的发酵时间对于分解那些难以消化的可发酵碳水化合物十分重要。

paragraphs.text: Before the Grand Manner era, a few significant gardens were found in Britain which were developed under the influence of the continent. Britain's homegrown domestic gardening traditions were mostly practical in purpose, rather than aesthetic, unlike the grand Gardens found mostly on castle grounds and less commonly in universities. Tudor Gardens emphasized contrast rather than transitions, distinguished by color and illusion. They were not intended as a complement to home or architecture, but conceived as independent spaces, arranged to grow and display flowers and ornamental plants. Gardeners demonstrated their artistry in knot gardens, with complex arrangements most commonly included interwoven box hedges, and less commonly fragrant herbs like rosemary. Sanded paths run between the hedgings of open knots whereas closed knots were filled with single-colored flowers.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 在宏伟风格时代之前，英国只有少数几座重要的园林，它们是在欧洲大陆的影响下发展起来的。英国本土的家庭园艺传统大多以实用为目的，而非追求美观，这与多见于城堡庭院、偶见于大学的宏伟园林不同。都铎式园林强调对比而非过渡，以色彩和视觉错觉见长。这类园林并非旨在衬托住宅或建筑，而是被设计为独立的空间，用以栽种和展示花卉与观赏植物。园丁们在绳结花园中展现技艺，其复杂的布局最常用交错的黄杨树篱，偶尔也会用迷迭香等芳香草本。在开放式绳结花园中，铺沙的小径穿行于树篱之间，而封闭式绳结花园则种满了单一色彩的花卉。

paragraphs.text: Cocoa beans are the processed seeds of the cacao tree. They are usually fermented to develop the flavor, then dried, cleaned, and roasted. The shell is removed to reveal nibs, which are ground to chocolate liquor The liquor can be further processed into cocoa butter and cocoa powder, or shaped and sold as unsweetened baking chocolate. By adding sugar, sweetened chocolates are produced, which can be sold simply as dark chocolate, or, with the addition of milk, can be made into milk chocolate.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 可可豆是经过加工的可可树种子。它们通常先经过发酵以形成风味，然后再干燥、清洗和烘烤。去除外壳后露出可可碎粒，将其研磨成可可液块；可可液块可进一步加工成可可脂和可可粉，或塑形后作为无糖烘焙巧克力出售。加入糖后便制成了加糖巧克力，它可以直接作为黑巧克力出售，也可以再加入牛奶制成牛奶巧克力。

paragraphs.text: A complete virus particle, known as a virion, consists of nucleic acid surrounded by a protective coat of protein called a capsid. These are formed from protein subunits called capsomeres. Viruses can have a lipid "envelope" derived from the host cell membrane. The capsid is made from proteins encoded by the viral genome and its shape serves as the basis for morphological distinction. Virally-coded protein subunits will self-assemble to form a capsid, in general requiring the presence of the virus genome. Complex viruses code for proteins that assist in the construction of their capsid.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 一个完整的病毒颗粒称为病毒粒子，由核酸以及包裹在其外的蛋白质保护外壳构成，这层外壳称为衣壳。衣壳由称为壳粒的蛋白质亚基构成。病毒可以具有一层源自宿主细胞膜的脂质包膜。衣壳由病毒基因组编码的蛋白质构成，其形状是病毒形态学区分的依据。由病毒编码的蛋白质亚基会自我组装形成衣壳，这一过程通常需要病毒基因组的存在。结构复杂的病毒还会编码一些蛋白质，以协助构建其衣壳。

paragraphs.text: The degree of roast affects coffee flavor and body. The color of the coffee after brewing is also affected by the degree of roasting. Darker roasts are generally bolder because they have less fiber content and a more sugary flavor. Lighter roasts have a more complex and therefore perceived stronger flavor from aromatic oils and acids otherwise destroyed by longer roasting times. Roasting does not alter the amount of caffeine in the bean but does give less caffeine when the beans are measured by volume because the beans expand during roasting. A small amount of chaff is produced during roasting from the skin left on the seed after processing.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 烘焙程度会影响咖啡的风味和醇厚度。咖啡冲泡后的颜色也会受到烘焙程度的影响。深度烘焙的咖啡通常口感更浓烈，因为其纤维含量更低、糖香更突出。浅度烘焙的咖啡风味更复杂，因而尝起来更浓郁，这源于芳香油和酸性物质，而这些成分在较长的烘焙时间中会被破坏。烘焙不会改变咖啡豆中咖啡因的总量，但按体积量取时咖啡因含量会偏少，因为咖啡豆在烘焙过程中会膨胀。烘焙过程中会产生少量谷壳，它来自加工后残留在种子上的表皮。

paragraphs.text: Cocoa beans are the processed seeds of the cacao tree. They are usually fermented to develop the flavor, then dried, cleaned, and roasted. The shell is removed to reveal nibs, which are ground to chocolate liquor The liquor can be further processed into cocoa butter and cocoa powder, or shaped and sold as unsweetened baking chocolate. By adding sugar, sweetened chocolates are produced, which can be sold simply as dark chocolate, or, with the addition of milk, can be made into milk chocolate. Making chocolate with only cocoa butter and milk produces white chocolate.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 可可豆是经过加工的可可树种子。可可豆通常先经发酵以形成风味，然后干燥、清洁并烘焙。去除外壳后露出可可碎粒，将其研磨即得可可浆；可可浆可进一步加工成可可脂和可可粉，或塑形后作为无糖烘焙巧克力出售。加入糖后便制成甜味巧克力，既可直接作为黑巧克力出售，也可再加入牛奶制成牛奶巧克力。仅用可可脂和牛奶制作巧克力，则得到白巧克力。

paragraphs.text: In general, RNA viruses have smaller genome sizes than DNA viruses because of a higher error-rate when replicating, and have a maximum upper size limit. Beyond this, errors when replicating render the virus useless or uncompetitive. To compensate, RNA viruses often have segmented genomes, the genome is split into smaller molecules, thus reducing the chance that an error in a single-component genome will incapacitate the entire genome. In contrast, DNA viruses generally have larger genomes because of the high fidelity of their replication enzymes.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 总体而言，RNA病毒的基因组比DNA病毒小，这是因为它们复制时的出错率更高，且基因组存在一个上限。一旦超过这一上限，复制时产生的错误就会使病毒失去功能或丧失竞争力。为弥补这一点，RNA病毒常具有分段的基因组，即把基因组拆分成更小的分子，从而降低单个组分出错而使整个基因组失效的可能性。相比之下，DNA病毒的基因组通常较大，因为其复制酶具有很高的保真度。

paragraphs.text: The degree of roast affects coffee flavor and body. The color of the coffee after brewing is also affected by the degree of roasting. Darker roasts are generally bolder because they have less fiber content and a more sugary flavor. Lighter roasts have a more complex and therefore perceived stronger flavor from aromatic oils and acids otherwise destroyed by longer roasting times. Roasting does not alter the amount of caffeine in the bean but does give less caffeine when the beans are measured by volume because the beans expand during roasting. A small amount of chaff is produced during roasting from the skin left on the seed after processing. Chaff is usually removed from the seeds by air movement, though a small amount is added to dark roast coffees to soak up oils on the seeds.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 烘焙程度会影响咖啡的风味和醇厚度。冲泡后咖啡的颜色也会受烘焙程度的影响。深度烘焙的咖啡通常口感更浓烈，因为其纤维含量更低、糖香更突出。浅度烘焙的咖啡风味更复杂，因而尝起来更浓郁，这源于那些原本会被较长烘焙时间破坏掉的芳香油和酸性物质。烘焙不会改变咖啡豆中咖啡因的含量，但按体积量取时咖啡因会偏少，因为豆子在烘焙过程中会膨胀。烘焙过程中，加工后残留在种子上的表皮会产生少量谷壳。谷壳通常借助气流从种子上除去，不过会在深度烘焙咖啡中加入少量谷壳，以吸收豆子表面的油脂。

paragraphs.text: There are around 450 official dog breeds, the most of any mammal. Dogs began diversifying in the Victorian era, when humans took control of their natural selection. Most breeds were derived from small numbers of founders within the last 200 years. Since then, dogs have undergone rapid phenotypic change and have been subjected to artificial selection by humans. The skull, body, and limb proportions between breeds display more phenotypic diversity than can be found within the entire order of carnivores.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 目前约有450个正式认可的犬种，是所有哺乳动物中最多的。狗在维多利亚时代开始分化，当时人类掌控了它们的自然选择。大多数犬种是在过去200年间由少量奠基个体培育而来的。自那以后，狗经历了快速的表型变化，并受到人类的人工选择。不同犬种之间在头骨、躯体和四肢比例上展现出的表型多样性，超过了整个食肉目动物所具有的多样性。

paragraphs.text: In Greek literature, there are numerous mentions of ariston, a meal taken not long after sunrise. The Iliad notes this meal with regard to a labor-weary woodsman eager for a light repast to start his day, preparing it even as he is aching with exhaustion. The opening prose of the 16th book of the Odyssey mentions breakfast as the meal being prepared in the morning before attending to one's chores. Eventually ariston was moved to around noon, and a new morning meal was introduced.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 在希腊文学中，多次提到“阿里斯顿”，这是一种在日出后不久食用的餐食。《伊利亚特》在提到这一餐时，描写了一位劳累的樵夫，他渴望以一顿便餐开始一天，尽管疲惫得浑身酸痛仍在准备这顿饭。《奥德赛》第十六卷的开篇散文提到，早餐是在早晨着手干活之前准备的一餐。最终，“阿里斯顿”被移到正午前后，一种新的晨间餐食随之出现。

paragraphs.text: The next step in the process is the roasting of the green coffee. Coffee is usually sold in a roasted state, and with rare exceptions, such as infusions from green coffee beans, coffee is roasted before it is consumed. It can be sold roasted by the supplier, or it can be home roasted. The roasting process influences the taste by changing the coffee bean physically and chemically. The bean decreases in weight as moisture is lost and increases in volume, causing it to become less dense.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 制作过程的下一步是烘焙生咖啡豆。咖啡通常以烘焙后的状态出售,除少数例外情况(例如用生咖啡豆冲泡)外,咖啡在饮用前都会经过烘焙。咖啡豆可以由供应商烘焙后出售,也可以在家中自行烘焙。烘焙过程通过改变咖啡豆的物理和化学性质来影响味道。随着水分流失,咖啡豆重量减轻、体积增大,因而密度降低。

paragraphs.text: The earliest modern-format "restaurants" to use that word in Paris were the establishments which served bouillon, a broth made of meat and egg which was said to restore health and vigour. The first restaurant of this kind was opened in 1765 or 1766 by Mathurin Roze de Chantoiseau on rue des Poulies, now part of the Rue de Louvre. The name of the owner is sometimes given as Boulanger. Unlike earlier eating places, it was elegantly decorated, and besides meat broth offered a menu of several other "restorative" dishes, including macaroni. Chantoiseau and other chefs took the title "traiteurs-restaurateurs". While not the first establishment where one could order food, or even soups, it is thought to be the first to offer a menu of available choices.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 巴黎最早采用"餐馆"这一称呼的现代形式店铺,是供应清汤的场所;这种清汤由肉和蛋熬制而成,据说能恢复健康与活力。第一家这类餐馆于1765年或1766年由马蒂兰·罗兹·德·尚图瓦索开设在普利耶街,该街如今已并入卢浮街。这家店主人的名字有时被记作布朗热。与早先的餐饮场所不同,这家店装潢雅致,除肉汤外还提供包含通心粉在内的多道其他"滋补"菜肴。尚图瓦索和其他厨师采用了"餐饮经营者"这一头衔。虽然它并非第一家可以点餐、甚至点汤的店铺,但一般认为它是第一家提供备选菜单的餐馆。

paragraphs.text: An umbrella is a folding canopy supported by wooden or metal ribs that is mounted on a wooden, metal, or plastic pole. It is usually designed to protect a person against sun or rain. Initially, they were used in warmer countries for shade from the sun, but in modern times they evolved to also be used for protection from rain. Etymologically, the term umbrella is to be used when protecting from the sun, but is also commonly used when protecting from rain. Some countries specifically use the words parasol and parapluie to differentiate based on their use. There are also combinations of parasol and parapluie that are called en-tout-cas.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 雨伞是一种可折叠的顶篷,由木制或金属伞骨支撑,并安装在木制、金属或塑料的伞杆上。雨伞通常设计用来为人遮挡阳光或雨水。起初,雨伞在气候较暖的国家用于遮挡阳光,但到了现代,它逐渐也被用来挡雨。从词源上看,"伞"这个词本应用于遮阳的场合,但如今也常用于指挡雨的伞具。一些国家会专门用不同的词语来区分遮阳伞和挡雨伞这两种用途。此外还有兼具遮阳和挡雨功能的组合伞,被称为晴雨两用伞。

paragraphs.text: Before the Grand Manner era, a few significant gardens were found in Britain which were developed under the influence of the continent. Britain's homegrown domestic gardening traditions were mostly practical in purpose, rather than aesthetic, unlike the grand Gardens found mostly on castle grounds and less commonly in universities. Tudor Gardens emphasized contrast rather than transitions, distinguished by color and illusion. They were not intended as a complement to home or architecture, but conceived as independent spaces, arranged to grow and display flowers and ornamental plants. Gardeners demonstrated their artistry in knot gardens, with complex arrangements most commonly included interwoven box hedges, and less commonly fragrant herbs like rosemary.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 在宏伟风格时代之前，英国已有少数颇具规模的花园，它们是在欧洲大陆的影响下发展起来的。英国本土的家庭园艺传统大多以实用而非审美为目的，这与多见于城堡庭园、偶见于大学的宏大花园截然不同。都铎风格花园强调对比而非过渡，以色彩和视觉错觉为特色。这类花园并非用来衬托住宅或建筑，而是被设计成独立的空间，用以栽种和展示花卉与观赏植物。园丁们在结纹花园中展现技艺，其复杂的布局最常用交错的黄杨绿篱构成，偶尔也会用迷迭香等芳香草本植物。

paragraphs.text: Each airport hires its own ground crew to process flights, cargo, passengers, and baggage. When a flight arrives, ramp services navigate a baggage cart to the aircraft. Bags are then sorted and transported by baggage handlers. Ramp services handle aircraft marshaling, the process in which aircraft arrive or depart from the gate. Once passengers and/or cargo is unloaded, a cleaning crew prepares the aircraft for its next flight by loading supplies and preparing other in-flight services. Ground crew will fuel the aircraft, and other visual inspections are conducted.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 每座机场都会雇佣自己的地勤人员，来处理航班、货物、旅客和行李。当航班抵达时，停机坪服务人员会将行李车驶往飞机旁。随后，行李由搬运人员进行分拣和运送。停机坪服务负责飞机的引导指挥，也就是飞机进出登机口的过程。一旦旅客和货物卸下，清洁人员便会装载补给、准备其他机上服务，为飞机的下一次航班做好准备。地勤人员会为飞机加注燃油，同时还会进行其他目视检查。

paragraphs.text: An umbrella is a folding canopy supported by wooden or metal ribs that is mounted on a wooden, metal, or plastic pole. It is usually designed to protect a person against sun or rain. Initially, they were used in warmer countries for shade from the sun, but in modern times they evolved to also be used for protection from rain. Etymologically, the term umbrella is to be used when protecting from the sun, but is also commonly used when protecting from rain. Some countries specifically use the words parasol and parapluie to differentiate based on their use. There are also combinations of parasol and parapluie that are called en-tout-cas. A modern hand-held umbrella or parasol may have a black exterior canopy and a silver inner coating, for better protection from both the sun and ultraviolet rays, and may be water-resistant.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 伞是一种可折叠的伞面，由木质或金属伞骨支撑，安装在木质、金属或塑料的伞杆上。它通常被设计用来为人遮挡阳光或雨水。最初，伞在气候较暖的国家用于遮挡阳光，但到了现代，它逐渐也被用来遮雨。从词源上讲，“伞”这个词原本指遮阳时所用的器具，但如今也常用来指遮雨的器具。有些国家专门用不同的词语来分别称呼遮阳伞和雨伞，以便根据用途加以区分。还有一种兼具遮阳与遮雨功能的组合，被称为晴雨两用伞。现代的手持雨伞或遮阳伞可能采用黑色的外层伞面和银色的内层涂层，以便更好地阻挡阳光和紫外线，而且可能还具有防水功能。

paragraphs.text: Depending on the color of the roasted beans as perceived by the human eye, they will be labeled as light, medium light, medium, medium dark, dark, or very dark. A more accurate method of discerning the degree of roast involves measuring the reflected light from roasted seeds illuminated with a light source in the near-infrared spectrum. This elaborate light meter uses a process known as spectroscopy to return a number that consistently indicates the roasted coffee's relative degree of roast or flavor development.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 根据人眼所感知的烘焙豆颜色，它们会被标注为浅度、中浅度、中度、中深度、深度或极深度。一种更精确判断烘焙程度的方法，是用近红外光谱的光源照射烘焙后的咖啡豆，并测量其反射光。这种精密的光度计利用一种称为光谱分析的方法，得出一个数值，稳定地反映烘焙咖啡的相对烘焙程度或风味发展水平。

paragraphs.text: Each airport hires its own ground crew to process flights, cargo, passengers, and baggage. When a flight arrives, ramp services navigate a baggage cart to the aircraft. Bags are then sorted and transported by baggage handlers. Ramp services handle aircraft marshaling, the process in which aircraft arrive or depart from the gate. Once passengers and/or cargo is unloaded, a cleaning crew prepares the aircraft for its next flight by loading supplies and preparing other in-flight services. Ground crew will fuel the aircraft, and other visual inspections are conducted. Efficient work allows aircraft to have fast turnaround times of as little as 25 minutes.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 每个机场都会雇用自己的地勤人员，处理航班、货物、旅客和行李。当航班抵达时，停机坪服务人员会将行李车驶向飞机。随后，行李由行李搬运员分拣并运送。停机坪服务负责飞机引导，即飞机进出登机口的过程。旅客和货物卸下后，清洁人员会装载补给并准备其他机上服务，为飞机的下一次航班做好准备。地勤人员会为飞机加油，同时进行其他目视检查。高效的作业使飞机的周转时间最短可缩至25分钟。

paragraphs.text: A study by the University of Hohenheim found that industrially produced bread typically has a high proportion of FODMAP carbohydrates due to a short rising time.
