paragraphs.translation_zh: 数学是一门研究抽象概念的知识领域，这些概念包括数、几何图形、集合、函数和概率等。它运用逻辑推理和证明来研究并确立这些概念的性质，这些性质常以定理、公式和方程的形式表达。数学被用来在科学、工程、技术、经济乃至日常生活中建立模型并解决问题。数学有许多分支，包括数论、代数、几何、分析和集合论。数学涉及对抽象对象的描述与处理，这些对象要么是从自然界中抽象出来的，要么是被规定具有某些性质的纯抽象实体，而这些被规定的性质被称为公理。数学运用纯粹的理性，通过证明来确立对象的性质，而证明是把演绎规则接连应用于已确立结论的一系列过程。这些被称为定理的结论，包括先前已证明的定理、公理，以及在从自然界抽象时被视为所讨论理论之真起点的某些基本性质。数学在自然科学、工程、医学、金融、计算机科学和社会科学中都不可或缺。尽管数学被广泛用于为经验现象建立模型，但它的结论是通过演绎证明而非实验来确立的。数学真理、逻辑与现实之间的关系是哲学争论的一个主题。某些数学分支，例如博弈论，是与其应用紧密结合发展起来的，通常被归入应用数学。另一些分支则独立于任何应用而发展，但往往在日后找到实际用途。

paragraphs.text: Many media scholars have discussed non-commercial news media with public service obligations as a means to improve the democratic process by providing the kind of political contents that a free market does not provide. The World Bank has recommended public service broadcasting in order to strengthen democracy in developing countries. These broadcasting services should be accountable to an independent regulatory body that is adequately protected from interference from political and economic interests. Public service media have an obligation to provide reliable information to voters. Many countries have publicly funded radio and television stations with public service obligations, especially in Europe and Japan, while such media are weak or non-existent in other countries including the US. Public service broadcasters are characterized by more policy-relevant information and more respect for journalistic norms and impartiality than the commercial media. However, the trend of deregulation has put the public service model under increased pressure from competition with commercial media. The emergence of the internet and the social media has profoundly altered the conditions for political communication. The social media have given ordinary citizens easy access to voice their opinion and share information while bypassing the filters of the large news media.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 许多媒体学者讨论过承担公共服务义务的非商业新闻媒体，认为它们能提供自由市场无法提供的那类政治内容，从而改善民主进程。世界银行曾建议发展公共服务广播，以加强发展中国家的民主。这些广播服务应当对一个独立的监管机构负责，而该机构应得到充分保护，免受政治和经济利益的干预。公共服务媒体有义务向选民提供可靠的信息。许多国家设有承担公共服务义务、由公共资金资助的广播电台和电视台，在欧洲和日本尤为如此，而在包括美国在内的另一些国家，这类媒体则薄弱甚至不存在。与商业媒体相比，公共服务广播机构的特点是提供更多与政策相关的信息，也更尊重新闻规范和公正性。然而，放松管制的趋势使公共服务模式在与商业媒体的竞争中承受着越来越大的压力。互联网和社交媒体的出现，深刻改变了政治传播的环境。社交媒体让普通公民能够轻松地表达意见、分享信息，同时绕过大型新闻媒体的过滤。

paragraphs.text: Respiration is the opposite of photosynthesis. It reclaims the energy to power chemical reactions in cells. In so doing the carbon atoms and their electrons are combined with oxygen forming CO2 which is easily removed from both the cells and the organism. Plants use both processes, photosynthesis to capture the energy and oxidative metabolism to use it. Plant respiration is limited by the process of diffusion. Plants take in carbon dioxide through holes, known as stomata, that can open and close on the undersides of their leaves and sometimes other parts of their anatomy. Most plants require some oxygen for catabolic processes. But the quantity of O2 used per hour is small as they are not involved in activities that require high rates of aerobic metabolism. Their requirement for air, however, is very high as they need CO2 for photosynthesis, which constitutes only 0. 04% of the environmental air. Thus, to make 1 g of glucose requires the removal of all the CO2 from at least 18. 7 liters of air at sea level. But inefficiencies in the photosynthetic process cause considerably greater volumes of air to be used.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 呼吸作用是光合作用的逆过程。呼吸作用重新释放能量，为细胞内的化学反应提供动力。在此过程中，碳原子及其电子与氧结合，生成二氧化碳，而二氧化碳很容易从细胞和生物体内排出。植物同时进行这两种过程：用光合作用捕获能量，用氧化代谢来利用能量。植物的呼吸作用受到扩散过程的限制。植物通过叶片背面、有时也通过身体其他部位上可开合的小孔吸入二氧化碳，这些小孔称为气孔。大多数植物的分解代谢过程都需要一定的氧气。但由于植物并不从事需要高速有氧代谢的活动，它们每小时消耗的氧气量很小。然而，植物对空气的需求量却很大，因为光合作用需要二氧化碳，而二氧化碳在空气中所占比例极低。仅占环境空气的0.04%。因此，要制造1克葡萄糖，至少需要把相当大量空气中的二氧化碳全部提取出来。在海平面，这一空气量约为18.7升。但由于光合过程存在效率损失，实际所需的空气量要大得多。

paragraphs.text: Epidemiology is used to break the chain of infection in populations during outbreaks of viral diseases. Control measures are used that are based on knowledge of how the virus is transmitted. It is important to find the source, or sources, of the outbreak and to identify the virus. Once the virus has been identified, the chain of transmission can sometimes be broken by vaccines. When vaccines are not available, sanitation and disinfection can be effective. Often, infected people are isolated from the rest of the community, and those that have been exposed to the virus are placed in quarantine. To control the outbreak of foot-and-mouth disease in cattle in Britain in 2001, thousands of cattle were slaughtered. Most viral infections of humans and other animals have incubation periods during which the infection causes no signs or symptoms. Incubation periods for viral diseases range from a few days to weeks, but are known for most infections. Somewhat overlapping, but mainly following the incubation period, there is a period of communicability, a time when an infected individual or animal is contagious and can infect another person or animal.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 流行病学被用来在病毒性疾病暴发期间切断人群中的感染链。人们会根据对病毒传播方式的了解采取相应的控制措施。找出疫情暴发的一个或多个源头并鉴定出病毒，是至关重要的。一旦确定了病毒，有时就可以借助疫苗切断传播链。在没有疫苗的情况下，搞好卫生和进行消毒也能起到效果。通常，会将感染者与社区其他人隔离开来，并对接触过病毒的人采取检疫隔离。为控制2001年英国牛群口蹄疫的暴发，数千头牛被宰杀。人类和其他动物的大多数病毒感染都有潜伏期，在此期间感染不会引起任何体征或症状。病毒性疾病的潜伏期从几天到数周不等，但大多数感染的潜伏期都是已知的。传染期与潜伏期略有重叠，但主要在潜伏期之后，在这段时间里，受感染的个体或动物具有传染性，能够把病毒传给另一个人或动物。

paragraphs.text: Natural selection can act at different levels of organisation, such as genes, cells, individual organisms, groups of organisms and species. Selection can act at multiple levels simultaneously. An example of selection occurring below the level of the individual organism are genes called transposons, which can replicate and spread throughout a genome. Selection at a level above the individual, such as group selection, may allow the evolution of cooperation. Genetic drift is the random fluctuation of allele frequencies within a population from one generation to the next. When selective forces are absent or relatively weak, allele frequencies are equally likely to drift upward or downward in each successive generation because the alleles are subject to sampling error. This drift halts when an allele eventually becomes fixed, either by disappearing from the population or by replacing the other alleles entirely. Genetic drift may therefore eliminate some alleles from a population due to chance alone. Even in the absence of selective forces, genetic drift can cause two separate populations that begin with the same genetic structure to drift apart into two divergent populations with different sets of alleles. According to the neutral theory of molecular evolution most evolutionary changes are the result of the fixation of neutral mutations by genetic drift. In this model, most genetic changes in a population are thus the result of constant mutation pressure and genetic drift. This form of the neutral theory has been debated since it does not seem to fit some genetic variation seen in nature. A better-supported version of this model is the nearly neutral theory, according to which a mutation that would be effectively neutral in a small population is not necessarily neutral in a large population. Other theories propose that genetic drift is dwarfed by other stochastic forces in evolution, such as genetic hitchhiking, also known as genetic draft. The time it takes a neutral allele to become fixed by genetic drift depends on population size; fixation is more rapid in smaller populations. The number of individuals in a population is not critical, but instead a measure known as the effective population size. The effective population is usually smaller than the total population since it takes into account factors such as the level of inbreeding and the stage of the lifecycle in which the population is the smallest.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 自然选择可以在不同的组织层级上发挥作用，例如基因、细胞、个体生物、生物群体以及物种。选择可以同时在多个层级上发挥作用。在个体生物层级之下发生选择的一个例子是被称为转座子的基因，它们能够在整个基因组中复制和扩散。在个体层级之上的选择，例如群体选择，可能促成合作行为的进化。遗传漂变是指种群中等位基因频率在世代之间的随机波动。当选择压力缺失或相对较弱时，由于等位基因受到抽样误差的影响，等位基因频率在每一代向上或向下漂移的可能性是相等的。当某个等位基因最终被固定下来——即从种群中消失，或完全取代其他等位基因——这种漂变便会停止。因此，遗传漂变可能仅凭偶然就将某些等位基因从种群中清除。即使没有选择压力，遗传漂变也能使两个起初具有相同遗传结构的独立种群逐渐分化，成为拥有不同等位基因组合的两个相异种群。根据分子进化的中性理论，大多数进化变化都是中性突变通过遗传漂变被固定的结果。因此在该模型中，种群中大多数遗传变化都是持续的突变压力和遗传漂变共同作用的结果。这种形式的中性理论一直存在争议，因为它似乎无法解释自然界中观察到的某些遗传变异。该模型有一个得到更多支持的版本，即近中性理论；按照这一理论，在小种群中实际上呈中性的突变，在大种群中未必是中性的。另一些理论则认为，遗传漂变在进化中被其他随机力量所掩盖，例如遗传搭车效应，也称为遗传牵连。中性等位基因通过遗传漂变被固定所需的时间取决于种群大小；在较小的种群中，固定发生得更快。起决定作用的并非种群中的个体数量，而是一个被称为有效种群大小的量度。有效种群通常小于总种群，因为它考虑了诸如近亲繁殖程度以及种群数量最少的生命周期阶段等因素。

paragraphs.text: The idea of a body so massive that even light could not escape was first proposed in the late 18th century by English astronomer and clergyman John Michell and independently by French scientist Pierre-Simon Laplace. Both scholars proposed very large stars in contrast to the modern concept of an extremely dense object. Michell correctly hypothesized that such non-radiating bodies might be detectable through their gravitational effects on nearby visible bodies. In 1905, Albert Einstein showed that the laws of electromagnetism are identical for observers travelling at different velocities relative to each other. The laws of mechanics had already been shown to be invariant in this way. However, the theory of gravitation was yet to be included. In 1907, Einstein published a paper proposing his equivalence principle, the hypothesis that inertial mass and gravitational mass have a common cause. Using the principle, Einstein predicted the redshift and the lensing effect of gravity on light; his prediction of gravitational lensing was one-half of the value that the full theory of general relativity would predict. By 1915, Einstein refined these ideas into his general theory of relativity, which explained how matter affects spacetime, which in turn affects the motion of other matter. This formed the basis for black hole physics. Only a few months after Einstein published the field equations describing general relativity, astrophysicist Karl Schwarzschild set out to apply the idea to stars. He assumed spherical symmetry with no spin and found a solution to Einstein's equations. At a certain radius from the centre of the mass, the Schwarzschild solution became singular, meaning that some of the terms in the Einstein equations became infinite. The nature of this radius, which later became known as the Schwarzschild radius, was not understood at the time. Many physicists of the early 20th century were sceptical of the existence of black holes. In a 1926 popular science book, Arthur Eddington critiqued the idea of a star with mass compressed to its Schwarzschild radius as a flaw in the then-poorly-understood theory of general relativity. In 1939, Einstein used his theory of general relativity in an attempt to prove that black holes were impossible. His work relied on increasing pressure or increasing centrifugal force balancing the force of gravity so that the object would not collapse beyond its Schwarzschild radius.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 认为存在一种质量极大、连光都无法逃逸的天体,这一设想最早由英国天文学家兼牧师约翰·米歇尔在18世纪末提出,法国科学家皮埃尔-西蒙·拉普拉斯也独立提出了同样的设想。与现代认为黑洞是一种极度致密天体的概念不同,这两位学者设想的都是体积非常巨大的恒星。米歇尔正确地推测,这类不发光的天体或许可以通过它们对附近可见天体的引力作用而被探测到。1905年,阿尔伯特·爱因斯坦证明,对于以不同相对速度运动的观察者来说,电磁定律都是相同的。力学定律此前就已被证明具有这种不变性。然而,引力理论当时还未被纳入其中。1907年,爱因斯坦发表了一篇论文,提出了等效原理,即惯性质量和引力质量具有共同成因这一假说。爱因斯坦运用这一原理,预言了引力对光的红移效应和透镜效应;他对引力透镜的预测值,只有完整的广义相对论所预测数值的一半。到1915年,爱因斯坦把这些思想完善为广义相对论,它解释了物质如何影响时空,而时空又如何反过来影响其他物质的运动。这奠定了黑洞物理学的基础。就在爱因斯坦发表描述广义相对论的场方程仅几个月之后,天体物理学家卡尔·史瓦西便着手把这一理论应用于恒星。他假设恒星呈球对称且不自转,从而求得了爱因斯坦方程的一个解。在距质量中心某一半径处,史瓦西解出现了奇点,这意味着爱因斯坦方程中的某些项变成了无穷大。这一半径后来被称为史瓦西半径,但其本质在当时并未被人理解。20世纪初的许多物理学家对黑洞的存在持怀疑态度。在1926年的一本科普著作中,阿瑟·爱丁顿批评了恒星质量被压缩到其史瓦西半径之内这一设想,认为它是当时尚未被充分理解的广义相对论中的一个缺陷。1939年,爱因斯坦试图用他的广义相对论来证明黑洞不可能存在。他的论证依赖于不断增大的压力或离心力来平衡引力,从而使天体不会坍缩到其史瓦西半径之内。

paragraphs.text: The Polish counter-offensive to the west halted the German advance for several days, but it was outflanked and encircled by the Wehrmacht. Remnants of the Polish army broke through to besieged Warsaw. On 17 September 1939, two days after signing a cease-fire with Japan, the Soviet Union invaded Poland under the supposed pretext that the Polish state had ceased to exist. Despite the military defeat, Poland never surrendered; instead, it formed the Polish government-in-exile and a clandestine state apparatus remained in occupied Poland. A significant part of Polish military personnel evacuated to Romania and Latvia; many of them later fought against the Axis in other theatres of the war. The proposal was rejected and Hitler ordered an immediate offensive against France, which was postponed until the spring of 1940 due to bad weather. Finland refused to sign a similar pact and rejected ceding part of its territory to the Soviet Union. Meanwhile, German, Soviet political relations and economic co-operation gradually stalled, and both states began preparations for war. Denmark capitulated after six hours, and despite Allied support, Norway was conquered within two months. On the same day, Germany launched an offensive against France. The Germans carried out a flanking manoeuvre through the Ardennes region, which was mistakenly perceived by the Allies as an impenetrable natural barrier against armoured vehicles. The United Kingdom was able to evacuate a significant number of Allied troops from the continent by early June, although they had to abandon almost all their equipment. Eight days later France signed an armistice with Germany; it was divided into German and Italian occupation zones, and an unoccupied rump state under the Vichy Regime, which, though officially neutral, was generally aligned with Germany. France kept its fleet, which the United Kingdom attacked on 3 July in an attempt to prevent its seizure by Germany. The German strategic bombing offensive intensified with night attacks on London and other cities in the Blitz, but largely ended in May 1941 after failing to significantly disrupt the British war effort. Still, a large majority of the American public continued to oppose any direct military intervention in the conflict well into 1941. The United States started strategic planning to prepare for a full-scale offensive against Germany. The Tripartite Pact stipulated that any country, with the exception of the Soviet Union, that attacked any Axis Power would be forced to go to war against all three.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 波兰向西发动的反攻使德军的推进停滞了数日，但波军最终被德国国防军包抄并合围。波兰军队的残部突围，进入了被围困的华沙。1939年9月17日，在与日本签订停火协议两天后，苏联以波兰国家已不复存在为借口入侵了波兰。尽管军事上战败，波兰却从未投降；相反，它成立了波兰流亡政府，并在被占领的波兰境内保留了一套秘密的国家机构。大批波兰军事人员撤往罗马尼亚和拉脱维亚；其中许多人后来在其他战场上继续与轴心国作战。该提议遭到拒绝，希特勒随即下令立即进攻法国，但因天气恶劣，进攻被推迟到1940年春。芬兰拒绝签署类似的条约，也拒绝将部分领土割让给苏联。与此同时，德国与苏联的政治关系和经济合作逐渐陷入停顿，两国都开始为战争做准备。丹麦在六小时后投降；尽管有盟国的支援，挪威仍在两个月内被征服。同一天，德国对法国发动了进攻。德军取道阿登地区实施了一次侧翼包抄，而盟军误以为该地区是装甲车辆无法逾越的天然屏障。到六月初，英国得以将大批盟军从欧洲大陆撤出，尽管他们不得不丢弃几乎全部装备。八天后，法国与德国签署了停战协定；法国被划分为德国和意大利的占领区，以及一个由维希政权统治的未被占领的残存国家，后者虽名义上中立，却大体上倒向德国。法国保留了自己的舰队，英国于7月3日对其发动攻击，试图防止它被德国夺取。德国的战略轰炸攻势随着对伦敦及其他城市的夜间空袭（即伦敦大轰炸）而加剧，但在未能显著打乱英国的战争行动之后，于1941年5月基本结束。尽管如此，直到1941年，绝大多数美国民众仍然反对以任何形式直接军事介入这场冲突。美国开始进行战略规划，为对德国发动全面进攻做准备。三国公约规定，除苏联外，任何攻击轴心国成员的国家都将被迫同时与三国开战。

paragraphs.text: Leftover debris that never became planets congregated in regions such as the asteroid belt, Kuiper belt, and Oort cloud. Within 50 million years, the pressure and density of hydrogen in the center of the protostar became great enough for it to begin thermonuclear fusion. As helium accumulates at its core, the Sun is growing brighter; early in its main-sequence life its brightness was 70% that of what it is today. The temperature, reaction rate, pressure, and density increased until hydrostatic equilibrium was achieved: the thermal pressure counterbalancing the force of gravity. At this point, the Sun became a main-sequence star. Solar wind from the Sun created the heliosphere and swept away the remaining gas and dust from the protoplanetary disc into interstellar space. Following the dissipation of the protoplanetary disk, the Nice model proposes that gravitational encounters between planetesimals and the gas giants caused each to migrate into different orbits. This led to dynamical instability of the entire system, which scattered the planetesimals and ultimately placed the gas giants in their current positions. During this period, the grand tack hypothesis suggests that a final inward migration of Jupiter dispersed much of the asteroid belt, leading to the Late Heavy Bombardment of the inner planets. The Solar System remains in a relatively stable, slowly evolving state by following isolated, gravitationally bound orbits around the Sun. Although the Solar System has been fairly stable for billions of years, it is technically chaotic, and may eventually be disrupted. There is a small chance that another star will pass through the Solar System in the next few billion years. The Sun's main-sequence phase, from beginning to end, will last about 10 billion years for the Sun compared to around two billion years for all other subsequent phases of the Sun's pre-remnant life combined. The Solar System will remain roughly as it is known today until the hydrogen in the core of the Sun has been entirely converted to helium, which will occur roughly 5 billion years from now. This will mark the end of the Sun's main-sequence life. At that time, the core of the Sun will contract with hydrogen fusion occurring along a shell surrounding the inert helium, and the energy output will be greater than at present. The outer layers of the Sun will expand to roughly 260 times its current diameter, and the Sun will become a red giant.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 那些始终未能形成行星的剩余碎屑，聚集在小行星带、柯伊伯带和奥尔特云等区域。在五千万年之内，原恒星中心氢的压强和密度变得足够大，使其开始发生热核聚变。随着氦在核心不断积聚，太阳正变得越来越亮；在其主序阶段早期，亮度只有如今的70%。温度、反应速率、压强和密度不断升高，直到达成流体静力平衡：热压力与引力相互抵消。至此，太阳成为一颗主序星。太阳发出的太阳风形成了日球层，并把原行星盘中残余的气体和尘埃吹散到星际空间。尼斯模型认为，在原行星盘消散之后，星子与气态巨行星之间的引力相互作用使它们各自迁移到了不同的轨道。这导致整个系统出现动力学不稳定，将星子四散抛出，并最终把气态巨行星安置在它们如今的位置。大迁徙假说认为，在此期间木星最后一次向内迁移，驱散了小行星带中的大部分天体，导致内行星遭受了晚期重轰炸。太阳系各天体沿着彼此独立、由引力束缚的轨道绕太阳运行，因而整体保持着相对稳定、缓慢演化的状态。尽管太阳系数十亿年来一直相当稳定，但严格说来它是混沌的，最终有可能被打破。在未来几十亿年里，另一颗恒星穿越太阳系的可能性虽小，但确实存在。太阳的主序阶段从头到尾将持续约100亿年，而太阳成为残骸之前其余所有后续阶段加起来才约20亿年。在太阳核心的氢全部转化为氦之前，太阳系将大体维持今天的样子，而这一转变大约会在50亿年后完成。这将标志着太阳主序阶段寿命的终结。到那时，太阳的核心将收缩，氢聚变将在包裹着惰性氦的一层壳层中进行，其能量输出将比现在更大。太阳的外层将膨胀到约为目前直径的260倍，太阳将变成一颗红巨星。

paragraphs.text: The Dutch government agreed to provide Japan with oil supplies from the Dutch East Indies, but negotiations for additional access to their resources ended in failure in June 1941. At the same time, Japan was planning an invasion of the Soviet Far East, intending to take advantage of the German invasion in the west, but abandoned the operation after the sanctions. Since early 1941, the United States and Japan had been engaged in negotiations in an attempt to improve their strained relations and end the war in China. Japan advanced a number of proposals which were dismissed by the Americans as inadequate. At the same time the United States, the United Kingdom, and the Netherlands engaged in secret discussions for the joint defence of their territories, in the event of a Japanese attack against any of them. Frustrated at the lack of progress and pressured by American, British, Dutch sanctions, especially in oil, Japan prepared for war. Emperor Hirohito, after initial hesitation about Japan's chances of victory, began to favour Japan's entry into the war. On 5 November, Hirohito approved in imperial conference the operations plan for the war. On 20 November, the new government presented an interim proposal as its final offer. It called for the end of American aid to China and for lifting the embargo on the supply of oil and other resources to Japan. In exchange, Japan promised not to launch any attacks in Southeast Asia and to withdraw its forces from southern Indochina. The American counter-proposal of 26 November required that Japan evacuate all of China without conditions and conclude non-aggression pacts with all Pacific powers. Japan planned to seize European colonies in Asia to create a large defensive perimeter stretching into the Central Pacific. The Japanese would then be free to exploit the resources of Southeast Asia while exhausting the over-stretched Allies by fighting a defensive war. Germany, followed by the other Axis states, declared war on the United States in solidarity with Japan, citing as justification the American attacks on German war vessels that had been ordered by Roosevelt. During 1942, Allied officials debated on the appropriate grand strategy to pursue. All agreed that defeating Germany was the primary objective. The Soviets demanded a second front. The British argued that military operations should target peripheral areas to wear out German strength, leading to increasing demoralisation, and bolstering resistance forces; Germany itself would be subject to a heavy bombing campaign.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 荷兰政府同意从荷属东印度向日本供应石油，但有关进一步获取当地资源的谈判于1941年6月以失败告终。与此同时，日本正计划入侵苏联远东地区，企图趁德国在西线发动进攻之机下手，但在遭受制裁后放弃了这一行动。自1941年初以来，美国和日本一直在进行谈判，试图改善紧张的双边关系并结束在中国的战争。日本提出了一系列方案，但都被美方以不够充分为由予以驳回。与此同时，美国、英国和荷兰就联合防卫各自领地展开了秘密磋商，以防日本对其中任何一方发动进攻。由于谈判毫无进展而倍感受挫，又迫于美、英、荷三国尤其是在石油方面的制裁压力，日本开始备战。裕仁天皇起初对日本的胜算心存犹豫，后来逐渐倾向于支持日本参战。11月5日，裕仁在御前会议上批准了这场战争的作战计划。11月20日，新政府提出了一份临时方案，作为其最后的提议。该方案要求美国停止援助中国，并解除对向日本供应石油及其他资源的禁运。作为交换，日本承诺不在东南亚发动任何进攻，并从印度支那南部撤军。美国在11月26日提出的反提案要求日本无条件撤出整个中国，并与所有太平洋国家缔结互不侵犯条约。日本计划夺取欧洲国家在亚洲的殖民地，以建立一道延伸至中太平洋的庞大防御圈。如此一来，日本便可放手攫取东南亚的资源，同时通过打一场防御战来拖垮战线过长的盟军。德国及随后其他轴心国为声援日本而对美国宣战，并以罗斯福下令美国攻击德国战舰为由替自己开脱。1942年间，盟国官员就应当采取何种恰当的总体战略展开了辩论。各方一致认为，击败德国是首要目标。苏联要求开辟第二战场。英国则主张军事行动应针对外围地区，以消耗德国的实力，使其士气日益低落，同时增强抵抗力量；至于德国本土，则要遭受大规模的轰炸。

paragraphs.text: Solar radiation modification is a proposal for reducing global warming by reflecting some sunlight away from Earth and back into space. Because it does not reduce greenhouse gas concentrations, it would not address ocean acidification and is not considered mitigation. SRM should be considered only as a supplement to mitigation, not a replacement for it, due to risks such as rapid warming if it were abruptly stopped and not restarted. The most-studied approach is stratospheric aerosol injection. SRM could reduce global warming and some of its impacts, though imperfectly. It poses environmental risks, such as changes to rainfall patterns, as well as political challenges, such as who would decide whether to use it. Renewable energy is key to limiting climate change. For decades, fossil fuels have accounted for roughly 80% of the world's energy use. The remaining share has been split between nuclear power and renewables. Fossil fuel use is expected to peak in absolute terms prior to 2030 and then to decline, with coal use experiencing the sharpest reductions. Renewables represented 86% of all new electricity generation installed in 2023. Other forms of clean energy, such as nuclear and hydropower, currently have a larger share of the energy supply. However, their future growth forecasts appear limited in comparison. While solar panels and onshore wind are now among the cheapest forms of adding new power generation capacity in many locations, green energy policies are needed to achieve a rapid transition from fossil fuels to renewables. To achieve carbon neutrality by 2050, renewable energy would become the dominant form of electricity generation, rising to 85% or more by 2050 in some scenarios. Investment in coal would be eliminated and coal use nearly phased out by 2050. Electricity generated from renewable sources would also need to become the main energy source for heating and transport. Transport can switch away from internal combustion engine vehicles and towards electric vehicles, public transit, and active transport. For shipping and flying, low-carbon fuels would reduce emissions. Heating could be increasingly decarbonized with technologies like heat pumps. There are obstacles to the continued rapid growth of clean energy, including renewables. Wind and solar produce energy intermittently and with seasonal variability. Traditionally, hydro dams with reservoirs and fossil fuel power plants have been used when variable energy production is low.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 太阳辐射调控是一项旨在通过把部分阳光反射离开地球、送回太空来减缓全球变暖的方案。由于它并不降低温室气体浓度，因此无法解决海洋酸化问题，也不被视为减缓措施。太阳辐射调控只应被当作减缓措施的补充，而非替代，因为它存在风险，比如一旦突然停止又不再重启，气温就会迅速回升。研究得最多的方法是向平流层注入气溶胶。太阳辐射调控能够减缓全球变暖及其部分影响，尽管效果并不完美。它带来了环境风险，比如改变降雨格局，也带来了政治难题，比如由谁来决定是否使用它。可再生能源是遏制气候变化的关键。数十年来，化石燃料一直占全球能源消耗的约80%。其余份额则由核电和可再生能源分摊。化石燃料的使用量预计将在2030年之前达到绝对峰值，随后下降，其中煤炭的用量降幅最大。2023年新增的发电装机容量中，可再生能源占了86%。核能和水电等其他形式的清洁能源，目前在能源供应中占有更大的份额。然而，相比之下，它们未来的增长预期显得有限。尽管在许多地方，太阳能电池板和陆上风电如今已是新增发电产能中最便宜的方式之一，但要实现从化石燃料向可再生能源的快速转型，仍需要绿色能源政策。要在2050年前实现碳中和，可再生能源将成为最主要的发电方式，在某些情景下到2050年其占比会升至85%或更高。对煤炭的投资将被取消，到2050年煤炭的使用也将几近淘汰。由可再生能源发出的电力还需要成为供暖和交通运输的主要能量来源。交通运输可以从内燃机车辆转向电动汽车、公共交通以及步行骑行等主动出行方式。对于航运和航空，低碳燃料有助于减少排放。供暖则可借助热泵等技术日益实现低碳化。包括可再生能源在内的清洁能源要持续快速增长，仍面临障碍。风能和太阳能发电具有间歇性，还会随季节变化。传统上，当这类波动性能源产量偏低时，会动用带水库的水电大坝和化石燃料发电厂来补充。

paragraphs.text: Puddling produced a structural grade iron at a relatively low cost. Puddling was backbreaking and extremely hot work. Few puddlers lived to be 40. Puddling became widely used after 1800. British iron manufacturers had used considerable amounts of iron imported from Sweden and Russia to supplement domestic supplies. Because of the increased British production, by the 1790s Britain eliminated imports and became a net exporter of bar iron. Hot blast, patented by the Scottish inventor James Beaumont Neilson in 1828, was the most important development of the 19th century for saving energy in making pig iron. The amount of fuel to make a unit of pig iron was reduced at first by between one-third using coke or two-thirds using coal; the efficiency gains continued as the technology improved. Hot blast raised the operating temperature of furnaces, increasing their capacity. Using less coal or coke meant introducing fewer impurities into the pig iron. This meant that lower quality coal could be used in areas where coking coal was unavailable or too expensive; however, by the end of the 19th century transportation costs fell considerably. Shortly before the Industrial Revolution, an improvement was made in the production of steel, which was an expensive commodity and used only where iron would not do, such as for cutting edge tools and springs. Benjamin Huntsman developed his crucible steel technique in the 1740s. The supply of cheaper iron and steel aided a number of industries, such as those making nails, hinges, wire, and other hardware items. The development of machine tools allowed better working of iron, causing it to be increasingly used in the rapidly growing machinery and engine industries. Smelting of copper in reverberatory furnaces using coal was pioneered in Bristol in the 1680s. Swansea in Britain developed in the 19th century into the World's prime hub of copper smelting importing ore from places like Chile, Cuba and Australia. This revolutionized Chilean copper mining to such degree that the country came to supply 19% of the copper produced worldwide in the 19th century. The development of the stationary steam engine was important in the Industrial Revolution; however, during its early period, most industrial power was supplied by water and wind. The first commercially successful industrial use of steam power was patented by Thomas Savery in 1698.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 搅炼法能以相对低廉的成本生产出结构级的铁。搅炼是一项极其繁重而又酷热难当的工作。很少有搅炼工能活到40岁。搅炼法在1800年之后得到广泛应用。英国的炼铁厂曾大量使用从瑞典和俄国进口的铁来补充国内供应。由于英国产量的提高，到18世纪90年代，英国不再进口，并成为条铁的净出口国。热风技术由苏格兰发明家詹姆斯·博蒙特·尼尔森于1828年取得专利，是19世纪在炼制生铁时节约能源方面最重要的进展。炼制单位生铁所需的燃料量，起初使用焦炭可减少约三分之一，使用煤则可减少约三分之二，而且随着技术改进，效率还在不断提高。热风提高了炉子的工作温度，从而增大了产能。使用更少的煤或焦炭，意味着带入生铁中的杂质也更少。这意味着在炼焦煤无法获得或过于昂贵的地区，可以改用质量较差的煤；不过到19世纪末，运输成本已大幅下降。就在工业革命前夕，钢的生产取得了一项改进，钢当时是一种昂贵的商品，只在铁无法胜任的地方才会使用，比如制作刀具的刃口和弹簧。本杰明·亨茨曼在18世纪40年代发明了他的坩埚钢工艺。更廉价的钢铁供应促进了多个行业的发展，例如生产钉子、铰链、金属丝及其他五金制品的行业。机床的发展使铁的加工更加精良，从而使铁在迅速发展的机械和发动机行业中得到越来越多的应用。用煤在反射炉中熔炼铜的做法，最早于17世纪80年代在布里斯托尔出现。英国的斯旺西在19世纪发展成为世界主要的铜冶炼中心，从智利、古巴和澳大利亚等地进口矿石。这彻底改变了智利的铜矿开采，以至于在19世纪，该国一度供应了全世界铜产量的19%。固定式蒸汽机的发展在工业革命中意义重大，然而在工业革命早期，大部分工业动力仍由水力和风力提供。蒸汽动力在工业上首次取得商业成功的应用，由托马斯·萨弗里于1698年取得专利。

paragraphs.text: The capacity and potential for humans to adapt is unevenly distributed across different regions and populations, and developing countries generally have less. The first two decades of the 21st century saw an increase in adaptive capacity in most low- and middle-income countries with improved access to basic sanitation and electricity, but progress is slow. Many countries have implemented adaptation policies. However, there is a considerable gap between necessary and available finance. Adaptation to sea level rise consists of avoiding at-risk areas, learning to live with increased flooding, and building flood controls. If that fails, managed retreat may be needed. There are economic barriers for tackling dangerous heat impact. Avoiding strenuous work or having air conditioning is not possible for everybody. In agriculture, adaptation options include a switch to more sustainable diets, diversification, erosion control, and genetic improvements for increased tolerance to a changing climate. Insurance allows for risk-sharing, but is often difficult to get for people on lower incomes. Education, migration and early warning systems can reduce climate vulnerability. Planting mangroves or encouraging other coastal vegetation can buffer storms. Ecosystems adapt to climate change, a process that can be supported by human intervention. By increasing connectivity between ecosystems, species can migrate to more favourable climate conditions. Species can also be introduced to areas acquiring a favourable climate. Protection and restoration of natural and semi-natural areas helps build resilience, making it easier for ecosystems to adapt. Many of the actions that promote adaptation in ecosystems, also help humans adapt via ecosystem-based adaptation. For instance, restoration of natural fire regimes makes catastrophic fires less likely, and reduces human exposure. Giving rivers more space allows for more water storage in the natural system, reducing flood risk. Restored forest acts as a carbon sink, but planting trees in unsuitable regions can exacerbate climate impacts. There are synergies but also trade-offs between adaptation and mitigation. An example for synergy is increased food productivity, which has large benefits for both adaptation and mitigation. An example of a trade-off is that increased use of air conditioning allows people to better cope with heat, but increases energy demand. Another trade-off example is that more compact urban development may reduce emissions from transport and construction, but may also increase the urban heat island effect, exposing people to heat-related health risks.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 人类适应气候变化的能力和潜力在不同地区和人群之间分布不均，发展中国家通常较为欠缺。21世纪头二十年，随着基本卫生设施和电力供应的改善，大多数中低收入国家的适应能力有所提升，但进展缓慢。许多国家已经实施了适应政策。然而，所需资金与可用资金之间存在相当大的缺口。应对海平面上升的适应措施包括避开高风险地区、学会与日益频繁的洪涝共处，以及修建防洪设施。如果这些措施失效，可能就需要有计划地撤离。在应对危险的高温影响方面存在经济上的障碍。避免从事繁重劳动或使用空调，并非人人都能做到。在农业方面，适应的选择包括转向更可持续的饮食、多样化经营、控制水土流失，以及通过遗传改良提高对气候变化的耐受力。保险可以分担风险，但低收入人群往往难以获得。教育、迁徙和预警系统能够降低对气候变化的脆弱性。种植红树林或扶持其他海岸植被，可以缓冲风暴的冲击。生态系统会适应气候变化，这一过程可以借助人类的干预加以促进。通过增强生态系统之间的连通性，物种能够迁移到气候条件更有利的地方。也可以把物种引入那些正逐渐获得适宜气候的地区。保护和恢复自然及半自然区域有助于增强韧性，使生态系统更易于适应。许多促进生态系统适应的举措，也通过基于生态系统的适应方式帮助人类适应。例如，恢复自然的火灾机制可以降低灾难性火灾发生的可能性，并减少人类的暴露风险。给河流留出更多空间，能让自然系统蓄存更多水量，从而降低洪涝风险。恢复后的森林可充当碳汇，但在不适宜的地区植树则可能加剧气候影响。适应与减缓之间既有协同，也存在权衡。协同的一个例子是提高粮食生产率，这对适应和减缓都有很大益处。权衡的一个例子是，更多使用空调能让人们更好地应对高温，却增加了能源需求。另一个权衡的例子是，更紧凑的城市开发或许能减少交通和建筑带来的排放，但也可能加剧城市热岛效应，使人们面临与高温相关的健康风险。

paragraphs.text: Gas lighting affected social and industrial organisation because it allowed factories and stores to remain open longer. Its introduction allowed nightlife to flourish in cities and towns as interiors and streets could be lighted on a larger scale than before. A new method of glass production, known as the cylinder process, was developed in Europe during the 19th century. In 1832 this process was used by the Chance Brothers to create sheet glass; they became the leading producers of window and plate glass. This advancement allowed for larger panes of glass to be created without interruption, thus freeing up the space planning in interiors as well as the fenestration of buildings. The Crystal Palace is a significant example of the use of sheet glass in a new and innovative structure. A machine for making a continuous sheet of paper, on a loop of wire fabric, was patented in 1798 by Louis-Nicolas Robert in France. The Fourdrinier machine is the predominant means of production today. The method of continuous production demonstrated by the paper machine influenced the development of continuous rolling of iron, steel and other continuous production processes. The British Agricultural Revolution raised crop yields and released labour for industrial employment, although per-capita food supply in much of Europe remained stagnant until the late 18th century. Key innovations included Jethro Tull's early 18th-century mechanical seed drill, which ensured more even sowing and depth control, Joseph Foljambe's iron Rotherham plough and Andrew Meikle's threshing machine, which reduced manual labour requirements. Coal mining in Britain started early. Before the steam engine, pits were often shallow bell pits following a seam of coal along the surface, which were abandoned as the coal was extracted. If the geology was favourable, the coal was mined by means of an adit or drift mine driven into the side of a hill. Shaft mining was done in some areas, but the limiting factor was the problem of removing water. It could be done by hauling buckets up the shaft or to a sough. The water had to be discharged into a stream or ditch at a level where it could flow away. Introduction of the steam pump by Thomas Savery in 1698 and the Newcomen steam engine in 1712 facilitated removal of water and enabled deeper shafts, enabling more coal to be extracted.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 煤气照明影响了社会与工业的组织方式,因为它使工厂和商店能够营业到更晚。煤气照明的引入让城镇的夜生活得以繁荣,因为室内和街道能够以前所未有的规模被照亮。一种被称为圆筒法的新制玻璃工艺,于19世纪在欧洲发展起来。1832年,钱斯兄弟公司用这一工艺制造平板玻璃,成为窗玻璃和厚平板玻璃的主要生产商。这一进步使得更大的玻璃板能够被连续不断地制造出来,从而为室内空间布局和建筑物的开窗设计带来了更大的自由。水晶宫是在新颖独创的结构中运用平板玻璃的一个重要范例。一种在环形金属网上连续抄造纸张的机器,于1798年由路易-尼古拉·罗贝尔在法国取得专利。长网造纸机是当今最主要的造纸设备。造纸机所展示的连续生产方式,影响了钢铁连续轧制以及其他连续生产工艺的发展。英国农业革命提高了作物产量,把劳动力释放出来用于工业就业,尽管在欧洲大部分地区,人均粮食供应直到18世纪晚期仍停滞不前。关键的革新包括杰思罗·塔尔在18世纪初发明的机械条播机,它使播种更均匀、深度更可控;还有约瑟夫·福尔詹姆的铁制罗瑟兰犁,以及安德鲁·米克尔的脱粒机,后者减少了对人力的需求。英国的采煤业起步很早。在蒸汽机出现之前,矿坑往往是沿着地表煤层开挖的浅钟形坑,煤一挖完便被废弃。如果地质条件有利,人们就开凿一条平硐或斜井深入山坡来采煤。有些地区采用竖井开采,但制约因素是排水的难题。排水可以通过沿竖井把水桶吊上来,或将水引向排水坑道来完成。积水必须排放到某一高度的溪流或沟渠中,使其能够自行流走。1698年托马斯·萨弗里引入蒸汽泵,1712年又出现了纽科门蒸汽机,它们便利了排水,使竖井得以挖得更深,从而能开采出更多的煤。

paragraphs.text: Albert Einstein's theory of general relativity, which describes gravitation as the curvature of spacetime, predicts that any sufficiently compact mass will form a black hole. The boundary of no escape is called the event horizon. In general relativity, crossing a black hole's event horizon traps an object inside but produces no locally detectable change. General relativity also predicts that every black hole should have a central singularity, where the curvature of spacetime is infinite. Objects whose gravitational fields are too strong for light to escape were first considered in the 18th century. In 1916, the first solution of general relativity that would characterise a black hole was found. By the late 1950s, this solution began to be interpreted physically as a region of space from which nothing can escape. Black holes were long considered a mathematical curiosity; it was not until the 1960s that theoretical work showed they were a generic prediction of general relativity. The first widely accepted black hole was Cygnus X-1, identified by several researchers independently in 1971. Black holes typically form when very massive stars collapse at the end of their life cycle. After a black hole has formed, it can grow by absorbing mass from its surroundings. Supermassive black holes of millions of solar masses may form by absorbing stars and merging with other black holes, or via direct collapse of gas clouds. There is consensus that supermassive black holes exist in the centres of most galaxies. Quantum field theory in curved spacetime predicts that event horizons emit Hawking radiation, with its rate of emission being inversely proportional to its mass. This causes the black hole to lose mass very slowly, provided it is not accreting matter. However, even the smallest class of black holes observed, stellar black holes, are gaining mass from the cosmic microwave background faster than they are losing mass via Hawking radiation. The presence of a black hole can be inferred through its interaction with matter and electromagnetic radiation such as visible light. Matter falling toward a black hole can form an accretion disk of infalling plasma, heated by friction and emitting light. In extreme cases, this creates a quasar, some of the brightest objects in the universe. Merging black holes can be detected by the gravitational waves they emit. If stars are orbiting a black hole, their motions can be used to determine the black hole's mass and location.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 阿尔伯特·爱因斯坦的广义相对论将引力描述为时空的弯曲，该理论预言任何足够致密的质量都会形成黑洞。这一无法逃逸的边界被称为事件视界。在广义相对论中，越过黑洞的事件视界会将物体困于其内，但不会产生任何在局部可探测到的变化。广义相对论还预言，每个黑洞都应有一个中心奇点，那里时空的曲率为无穷大。引力场强到连光都无法逃逸的天体，最早在18世纪就被设想过。1916年，人们找到了广义相对论中第一个能够描述黑洞的解。到20世纪50年代末，这个解开始在物理上被解释为一个任何东西都无法逃离的空间区域。黑洞长期被视为一种数学上的奇异现象；直到20世纪60年代，理论研究才表明它们是广义相对论的一个普遍预言。第一个被广泛接受的黑洞是天鹅座X-1，1971年由多位研究者各自独立确认。黑洞通常在质量极大的恒星于其生命周期末期坍缩时形成。黑洞形成之后，可以通过吸收周围的物质而增长。质量达数百万倍太阳质量的超大质量黑洞，可能通过吞噬恒星、与其他黑洞合并，或通过气体云的直接坍缩而形成。目前已达成共识：大多数星系的中心都存在超大质量黑洞。弯曲时空中的量子场论预言，事件视界会发出霍金辐射，其辐射速率与黑洞质量成反比。只要黑洞不再吸积物质，这便会使其极其缓慢地损失质量。然而，即使是观测到的最小一类黑洞——恒星级黑洞——从宇宙微波背景辐射中获取质量的速度，也快于它们通过霍金辐射损失质量的速度。黑洞的存在可以通过它与物质以及可见光等电磁辐射的相互作用来推断。落向黑洞的物质会形成一个由内落等离子体构成的吸积盘，等离子体因摩擦而被加热并发出光。在极端情况下，这会产生类星体，即宇宙中最明亮的天体之一。正在合并的黑洞可以通过它们发出的引力波被探测到。如果有恒星绕黑洞运行，可以利用它们的运动来确定黑洞的质量和位置。

paragraphs.text: The asteroid belt contains tens of thousands, possibly millions, of objects over one kilometer in diameter. Despite this, the total mass of the asteroid belt is unlikely to be more than a thousandth of that of Earth. The asteroid belt is very sparsely populated; spacecraft routinely pass through without incident. Below are the descriptions of the three largest bodies in the asteroid belt. They are all considered to be relatively intact protoplanets, a precursor stage before becoming a fully-formed planet: Ceres is the only dwarf planet in the asteroid belt. It is the largest object in the belt, with a diameter of 940 km. Its surface contains a mixture of carbon, frozen water and hydrated minerals. There are signs of past cryovolcanic activity, where volatile material such as water are erupted onto the surface, as seen in surface bright spots. Ceres has a very thin water vapor atmosphere, but practically speaking it is indistinguishable from a vacuum. Vesta is the second-largest object in the asteroid belt. Its fragments survive as the Vesta asteroid family and numerous HED meteorites found on Earth. Vesta's surface, dominated by basaltic and metamorphic material, has a denser composition than Ceres's. Its surface is marked by two giant craters: Rheasilvia and Veneneia. Pallas is the third-largest object in the asteroid belt. It has its own Pallas asteroid family. Not much is known about Pallas because it has never been visited by a spacecraft, though its surface is predicted to be composed of silicates. Hilda asteroids are in a 3:2 resonance with Jupiter; that is, they go around the Sun three times for every two Jovian orbits. They lie in three linked clusters between Jupiter and the main asteroid belt. Every planet except Mercury is known to possess at least one trojan. The Jupiter trojan population is roughly equal to that of the asteroid belt. After Jupiter, Neptune possesses the most confirmed trojans, at 28. The outer region of the Solar System is home to the giant planets and their large moons. The centaurs and many short-period comets orbit in this region. The four outer planets, called giant planets or Jovian planets, collectively make up 99% of the mass orbiting the Sun. All four giant planets have multiple moons and a ring system, although only Saturn's rings are easily observed from Earth.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 小行星带包含数万乃至可能数百万个直径超过一公里的天体。尽管如此，小行星带的总质量不太可能超过地球质量的千分之一。小行星带中天体分布极为稀疏，航天器经常穿越而平安无事。以下是对小行星带中三个最大天体的描述。它们都被认为是相对完整的原行星，处于成为完全成形行星之前的前体阶段：谷神星是小行星带中唯一的矮行星。谷神星是小行星带中最大的天体，直径为940公里。其表面含有碳、冰冻水和水合矿物的混合物。有迹象表明曾发生过冰火山活动，水等挥发性物质喷发到表面，这从表面的亮斑中可以看出。谷神星有一层极其稀薄的水蒸气大气，但实际上与真空难以区分。灶神星是小行星带中第二大的天体。它的碎块以灶神星族小行星的形式存留下来，还有许多在地球上发现的HED陨石。灶神星的表面以玄武岩质和变质物质为主，其成分比谷神星的更为致密。其表面有两个巨大的撞击坑：雷亚希尔维亚坑和韦内内亚坑。智神星是小行星带中第三大的天体。它拥有自己的智神星族小行星。由于从未有航天器造访智神星，人们对它了解不多，不过推测其表面由硅酸盐构成。希尔达小行星与木星处于3:2的共振关系；也就是说，木星每绕太阳两圈，它们就绕三圈。它们分布在木星与主小行星带之间三个相互连接的星群中。除水星外，已知每颗行星都至少拥有一颗特洛伊小行星。木星特洛伊小行星的数量大致与小行星带中的小行星数量相当。继木星之后，海王星拥有最多已确认的特洛伊小行星，共28颗。太阳系的外围区域是巨行星及其大型卫星的所在之处。半人马小行星和许多短周期彗星都在这一区域运行。四颗外行星被称为巨行星或类木行星，它们合计占了绕太阳运行物质质量的99%。这四颗巨行星都拥有多颗卫星和一套环系统，不过只有土星环易于从地球上观测到。

paragraphs.text: Global human-caused greenhouse gas emissions in 2019 were equivalent to 59 billion tonnes of CO2. Of these emissions, 75% was CO2, 18% was methane, 4% was nitrous oxide, and 2% was fluorinated gases. CO2 emissions primarily come from burning fossil fuels to provide energy for transport, manufacturing, heating, and electricity. Additional CO2 emissions come from deforestation and industrial processes, which include the CO2 released by the chemical reactions for making cement, steel, aluminium, and fertilizer. Methane emissions come from livestock, manure, rice cultivation, landfills, wastewater, and coal mining, as well as oil and gas extraction. Nitrous oxide emissions largely come from the microbial decomposition of fertilizer. While methane only lasts in the atmosphere for an average of 12 years, CO2 lasts much longer. The Earth's surface absorbs CO2 as part of the carbon cycle. While plants on land and in the ocean absorb most excess emissions of CO2 every year, that CO2 is returned to the atmosphere when biological matter is digested, burns, or decays. Land-surface carbon sink processes, such as carbon fixation in the soil and photosynthesis, remove about 29% of annual global CO2 emissions. The ocean has absorbed 20 to 30% of emitted CO2 over the last two decades. CO2 is only removed from the atmosphere for the long term when it is stored in the Earth's crust, which is a process that can take millions of years to complete. Around 30% of Earth's land area is largely unusable for humans, 26% is forests, 10% is shrubland and 34% is agricultural land. Deforestation is the main land use change contributor to global warming, as the destroyed trees release CO2, and are not replaced by new trees, removing that carbon sink. Between 2001 and 2018, 27% of deforestation was from permanent clearing to enable agricultural expansion for crops and livestock. Another 24% has been lost to temporary clearing under the shifting cultivation agricultural systems. 26% was due to logging for wood and derived products, and wildfires have accounted for the remaining 23%. Some forests have not been fully cleared, but were already degraded by these impacts. Restoring these forests also recovers their potential as a carbon sink. Local vegetation cover impacts how much of the sunlight gets reflected back into space, and how much heat is lost by evaporation.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 2019年全球由人类活动造成的温室气体排放量,相当于590亿吨二氧化碳。在这些排放中,二氧化碳占75%,甲烷占18%,氧化亚氮占4%,含氟气体占2%。二氧化碳排放主要来自燃烧化石燃料,以为交通、制造业、供暖和发电提供能源。另一部分二氧化碳排放来自森林砍伐和工业生产过程,其中包括制造水泥、钢铁、铝和化肥的化学反应所释放的二氧化碳。甲烷排放来自牲畜、粪肥、水稻种植、垃圾填埋场、废水和煤矿开采,以及石油和天然气开采。氧化亚氮排放主要来自微生物对化肥的分解。甲烷在大气中平均只能存留12年,而二氧化碳存留的时间要长得多。作为碳循环的一部分,地球表面会吸收二氧化碳。尽管陆地和海洋中的植物每年会吸收大部分多余的二氧化碳排放,但当生物物质被消化、燃烧或腐烂时,这些二氧化碳又会重新回到大气中。陆地表面的碳汇过程,例如土壤中的碳固定和光合作用,大约清除了全球每年二氧化碳排放量的29%。在过去二十年里,海洋吸收了所排放二氧化碳的20%至30%。只有当二氧化碳被封存于地壳之中时,它才会被长期清除出大气,而这一过程可能需要数百万年才能完成。地球陆地面积中,约有30%基本无法供人类利用,26%是森林,10%是灌木地,34%是农业用地。森林砍伐是导致全球变暖的主要土地利用变化因素,因为被毁的树木会释放二氧化碳,又没有新的树木来取代,从而失去了那部分碳汇。2001年至2018年间,27%的森林砍伐源于为发展种植业和畜牧业而进行的永久性清除。另有24%的森林因迁移耕作农业体系下的临时性清除而损失。26%是由于为获取木材及其衍生产品而进行的采伐,其余23%则由野火造成。有些森林虽未被完全清除,但已因这些影响而退化。恢复这些森林也能使它们重新具备作为碳汇的潜力。当地的植被覆盖会影响有多少阳光被反射回太空,以及有多少热量通过蒸发散失。

paragraphs.text: A black hole is an astronomical body so compact that its gravity prevents anything, including light, from escaping. Albert Einstein's theory of general relativity, which describes gravitation as the curvature of spacetime, predicts that any sufficiently compact mass will form a black hole. The boundary of no escape is called the event horizon. In general relativity, crossing a black hole's event horizon traps an object inside but produces no locally detectable change. General relativity also predicts that every black hole should have a central singularity, where the curvature of spacetime is infinite. Objects whose gravitational fields are too strong for light to escape were first considered in the 18th century. In 1916, the first solution of general relativity that would characterise a black hole was found. By the late 1950s, this solution began to be interpreted physically as a region of space from which nothing can escape. Black holes were long considered a mathematical curiosity; it was not until the 1960s that theoretical work showed they were a generic prediction of general relativity. The first widely accepted black hole was Cygnus X-1, identified by several researchers independently in 1971. Black holes typically form when very massive stars collapse at the end of their life cycle. After a black hole has formed, it can grow by absorbing mass from its surroundings. Supermassive black holes of millions of solar masses may form by absorbing stars and merging with other black holes, or via direct collapse of gas clouds. There is consensus that supermassive black holes exist in the centres of most galaxies. Quantum field theory in curved spacetime predicts that event horizons emit Hawking radiation, with its rate of emission being inversely proportional to its mass. This causes the black hole to lose mass very slowly, provided it is not accreting matter. However, even the smallest class of black holes observed, stellar black holes, are gaining mass from the cosmic microwave background faster than they are losing mass via Hawking radiation. The presence of a black hole can be inferred through its interaction with matter and electromagnetic radiation such as visible light. Matter falling toward a black hole can form an accretion disk of infalling plasma, heated by friction and emitting light. In extreme cases, this creates a quasar, some of the brightest objects in the universe.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 黑洞是一种极为致密的天体，其引力之强，使包括光在内的任何事物都无法逃逸。阿尔伯特·爱因斯坦的广义相对论将引力描述为时空的弯曲，并预言任何足够致密的质量都会形成黑洞。这一无法逃逸的边界称为事件视界。在广义相对论中，越过黑洞的事件视界会使物体被困于其内，但在局部却察觉不到任何变化。广义相对论还预言，每个黑洞中心都应有一个奇点，那里时空的曲率为无穷大。引力场强到连光都无法逃逸的天体，最早在18世纪就被人设想过。1916年，人们找到了广义相对论中第一个能够描述黑洞的解。到20世纪50年代末，这个解开始在物理上被解释为一个任何事物都无法逃离的空间区域。黑洞长期被视为数学上的奇异之物；直到20世纪60年代，理论研究才表明它们是广义相对论的一个普遍预言。第一个被广泛认可的黑洞是天鹅座X-1，它于1971年由多位研究者各自独立地确认。黑洞通常在质量极大的恒星于其生命周期末期发生坍缩时形成。黑洞形成之后，可以通过吸收周围的物质而不断增长。质量达数百万倍太阳质量的超大质量黑洞，可能通过吞噬恒星、与其他黑洞合并，或经由气体云的直接坍缩而形成。目前已形成共识：大多数星系的中心都存在超大质量黑洞。弯曲时空中的量子场论预言，事件视界会发出霍金辐射，其辐射速率与黑洞质量成反比。在不吸积物质的前提下，这会使黑洞极其缓慢地损失质量。然而，即便是观测到的最小一类黑洞——恒星级黑洞，从宇宙微波背景中获得质量的速度也快于通过霍金辐射损失质量的速度。黑洞的存在可以通过它与物质以及可见光等电磁辐射的相互作用来推断。朝黑洞坠落的物质会形成一个由内落等离子体组成的吸积盘，这些等离子体因摩擦而被加热并发出光。在极端情况下，这会形成类星体，即宇宙中最明亮的天体之一。

paragraphs.text: DNA is a long biopolymer composed of four types of bases. The sequence of bases along a particular DNA molecule specifies the genetic information, in a manner similar to a sequence of letters spelling out a sentence. Before a cell divides, the DNA is copied, so that each of the resulting two cells will inherit the DNA sequence. Portions of a DNA molecule that specify a single functional unit are called genes; different genes have different sequences of bases. Within cells, each long strand of DNA is called a chromosome. The specific location of a DNA sequence within a chromosome is known as a locus. If the DNA sequence at a locus varies between individuals, the different forms of this sequence are called alleles. DNA sequences can change through mutations, producing new alleles. If a mutation occurs within a gene, the new allele may affect the trait that the gene controls, altering the phenotype of the organism. However, while this simple correspondence between an allele and a trait works in some cases, most traits are influenced by multiple genes in a quantitative or epistatic manner. Evolution can occur if there is genetic variation within a population. Variation comes from mutations in the genome, reshuffling of genes through sexual reproduction and migration between populations. Despite the constant introduction of new variation through mutation and gene flow, most of the genome of a species is very similar among all individuals of that species. However, discoveries in the field of evolutionary developmental biology have demonstrated that even relatively small differences in genotype can lead to dramatic differences in phenotype both within and between species. An individual organism's phenotype results from both its genotype and the influence of the environment it has lived in. The modern evolutionary synthesis defines evolution as the change over time in this genetic variation. The frequency of one particular allele will become more or less prevalent relative to other forms of that gene. Variation disappears when a new allele reaches the point of fixation, when it either disappears from the population or replaces the ancestral allele entirely. Mutations are changes in the DNA sequence of a cell's genome and are the ultimate source of genetic variation in all organisms. When mutations occur, they may alter the product of a gene, or prevent the gene from functioning, or have no effect.
paragraphs.translation_zh: DNA是一种由四种碱基构成的长链生物聚合物。某条DNA分子上碱基的排列顺序决定了遗传信息，其方式类似于一串字母拼写出一个句子。在细胞分裂之前，DNA会被复制，这样分裂得到的两个细胞都会继承这段DNA序列。DNA分子中决定单个功能单元的片段称为基因；不同的基因具有不同的碱基序列。在细胞内，每一条长长的DNA链都称为一条染色体。某段DNA序列在染色体上的特定位置称为基因座。如果某个基因座上的DNA序列在不同个体之间存在差异，这些不同形式的序列就称为等位基因。DNA序列可以通过突变而改变，从而产生新的等位基因。如果突变发生在某个基因内部，新的等位基因可能影响该基因所控制的性状，从而改变生物体的表型。然而，等位基因与性状之间这种简单的对应关系在某些情况下成立，但大多数性状是受多个基因以数量遗传或上位效应的方式共同影响的。只要种群内部存在遗传变异，进化就可能发生。变异来源于基因组中的突变、有性生殖对基因的重新组合，以及种群之间的迁移。尽管突变和基因流不断引入新的变异，一个物种的基因组在该物种所有个体之间仍然大体相似。然而，进化发育生物学领域的发现表明，即使基因型上相对微小的差异，也可能在物种内部和物种之间造成表型上的巨大差异。一个生物体的表型既取决于它的基因型，也取决于它所生活环境的影响。现代进化综合论把进化定义为这种遗传变异随时间的变化。某一特定等位基因的频率，相对于该基因的其他形式，会变得或多或少地常见。当一个新的等位基因达到固定时，变异便消失，此时它要么从种群中消失，要么完全取代了原有的祖先等位基因。突变是细胞基因组DNA序列发生的改变，是所有生物体遗传变异的最终来源。突变发生时，可能改变某个基因的产物，可能使该基因无法发挥功能，也可能毫无影响。

paragraphs.text: When B or T cells encounter their related antigens they multiply and many "clones" of the cells are produced that target the same antigen. This is called clonal selection. Both B cells and T cells carry receptor molecules that recognize specific targets. T cells recognize a "non-self" target, such as a pathogen, only after antigens have been processed and presented in combination with a "self" receptor called a major histocompatibility complex molecule. There are two major subtypes of T cells: the killer T cell and the helper T cell. In addition there are regulatory T cells which have a role in modulating immune response. Killer T cells are a sub-group of T cells that kill cells that are infected with viruses, or are otherwise damaged or dysfunctional. As with B cells, each type of T cell recognizes a different antigen. Killer T cells are activated when their T-cell receptor binds to this specific antigen in a complex with the MHC Class I receptor of another cell. Recognition of this MHC:antigen complex is aided by a co-receptor on the T cell, called CD8. The T cell then travels throughout the body in search of cells where the MHC I receptors bear this antigen. When an activated T cell contacts such cells, it releases cytotoxins, such as perforin, which form pores in the target cell's plasma membrane, allowing ions, water and toxins to enter. The entry of another toxin called granulysin induces the target cell to undergo apoptosis. T cell killing of host cells is particularly important in preventing the replication of viruses. T cell activation is tightly controlled and generally requires a very strong MHC/antigen activation signal, or additional activation signals provided by "helper" T cells. Helper T cells regulate both the innate and adaptive immune responses and help determine which immune responses the body makes to a particular pathogen. These cells have no cytotoxic activity and do not kill infected cells or clear pathogens directly. They instead control the immune response by directing other cells to perform these tasks. The MHC:antigen complex is also recognized by the helper cell's CD4 co-receptor, which recruits molecules inside the T cell that are responsible for the T cell's activation. Helper T cell activation also requires longer duration of engagement with an antigen-presenting cell.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 当B细胞或T细胞遇到与之相应的抗原时，它们会大量增殖，产生许多针对同一抗原的细胞“克隆”。这被称为克隆选择。B细胞和T细胞都携带能够识别特定靶标的受体分子。只有当抗原经过加工，并与一种叫作主要组织相容性复合体分子的“自身”受体结合呈递之后，T细胞才能识别出病原体之类的“非自身”靶标。T细胞主要分为两大亚型：杀伤性T细胞和辅助性T细胞。此外还有调节性T细胞，它们在调控免疫应答方面发挥作用。杀伤性T细胞是T细胞的一个亚群，专门杀死被病毒感染的细胞，或其他受损、功能异常的细胞。与B细胞一样，每一种T细胞识别一种不同的抗原。当杀伤性T细胞的T细胞受体，与另一个细胞上同主要组织相容性复合体Ⅰ类受体结合在一起的这种特异性抗原相结合时，杀伤性T细胞就会被激活。对这种主要组织相容性复合体与抗原复合物的识别，由T细胞上一种称为CD8的辅助受体协助完成。随后，T细胞会在全身游走，寻找那些主要组织相容性复合体Ⅰ类受体携带这种抗原的细胞。当被激活的T细胞接触到这类细胞时，它会释放穿孔素等细胞毒素，在靶细胞的质膜上形成孔洞，使离子、水和毒素得以进入。另一种叫作颗粒溶素的毒素进入后，会诱导靶细胞发生凋亡。T细胞杀伤宿主细胞，对于阻止病毒复制尤为重要。T细胞的激活受到严格控制，通常需要非常强的主要组织相容性复合体与抗原激活信号，或由“辅助性”T细胞提供的额外激活信号。辅助性T细胞既调节先天免疫应答，也调节适应性免疫应答，并帮助决定身体针对某一特定病原体作出哪种免疫应答。这类细胞没有细胞毒活性，不会直接杀死受感染的细胞或清除病原体。相反，它们通过指挥其他细胞来执行这些任务，从而控制免疫应答。主要组织相容性复合体与抗原的复合物也会被辅助细胞的CD4辅助受体识别，后者招募T细胞内负责激活该T细胞的分子。辅助性T细胞的激活还需要与抗原呈递细胞保持更长时间的接触。

paragraphs.text: Developmental biologists suggest that complex interactions in genetic networks and communication among cells can lead to heritable variations that may underlay some of the mechanics in developmental plasticity and canalisation. Heritability may also occur at even larger scales. For example, ecological inheritance through the process of niche construction is defined by the regular and repeated activities of organisms in their environment. This generates a legacy of effects that modify and feed back into the selection regime of subsequent generations. Other examples of heritability in evolution that are not under the direct control of genes include the inheritance of cultural traits and symbiogenesis. From a neo-Darwinian perspective, evolution occurs when there are changes in the frequencies of alleles within a population of interbreeding organisms, for example, the allele for black colour in a population of moths becoming more common. Mechanisms that can lead to changes in allele frequencies include natural selection, genetic drift, and mutation bias. Evolution by natural selection is the process by which traits that enhance survival and reproduction become more common in successive generations of a population. It embodies three principles: Variation exists within populations of organisms with respect to morphology, physiology and behaviour. Different traits confer different rates of survival and reproduction. These traits can be passed from generation to generation. More offspring are produced than can possibly survive, and these conditions produce competition between organisms for survival and reproduction. Consequently, organisms with traits that give them an advantage over their competitors are more likely to pass on their traits to the next generation than those with traits that do not confer an advantage. This teleonomy is the quality whereby the process of natural selection creates and preserves traits that are seemingly fitted for the functional roles they perform. Consequences of selection include nonrandom mating and genetic hitchhiking. The central concept of natural selection is the evolutionary fitness of an organism. Fitness is measured by an organism's ability to survive and reproduce, which determines the size of its genetic contribution to the next generation. However, fitness is not the same as the total number of offspring: instead fitness is indicated by the proportion of subsequent generations that carry an organism's genes. If an allele increases fitness more than the other alleles of that gene, then with each generation this allele has a higher probability of becoming common within the population.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 发育生物学家认为，遗传网络中的复杂相互作用以及细胞间的信息交流，可以产生可遗传的变异，而这些变异可能是发育可塑性和发育稳态某些机制的基础。可遗传性还可能在更大的尺度上发生。例如，通过生态位构建过程实现的生态遗传，是由生物体在其环境中有规律的、反复的活动所界定的。这会留下一系列影响，改变后代所处的选择环境并对其形成反馈。进化中不受基因直接控制的可遗传性，还有文化性状的传承和共生起源等其他例子。从新达尔文主义的视角看，当一个相互交配的生物种群内等位基因的频率发生变化时，进化便会发生，例如蛾类种群中控制黑色的等位基因变得更为常见。可导致等位基因频率变化的机制包括自然选择、遗传漂变和突变偏向。自然选择驱动的进化，是指有利于生存和繁殖的性状在种群一代代延续中变得越来越普遍的过程。它包含三条原则：生物种群在形态、生理和行为方面存在变异。不同的性状带来不同的生存率和繁殖率。这些性状能够代代相传。所产生的后代数量超过了可能存活的数量，这种状况使生物之间为生存和繁殖而展开竞争。因此，拥有使其胜过竞争者的性状的生物，比那些性状不具优势的生物更有可能把自己的性状遗传给下一代。这种目的性是指：自然选择过程会创造并保留那些看似与其所承担功能相契合的性状。选择带来的结果包括非随机交配和遗传搭车效应。自然选择的核心概念是生物体的进化适合度。适合度以生物体生存和繁殖的能力来衡量，这种能力决定了它对下一代基因贡献的大小。然而，适合度并不等同于后代的总数，而是由后代中携带某一生物体基因的比例来体现的。如果某个等位基因比该基因的其他等位基因更能提高适合度，那么每经过一代，这个等位基因在种群中变得普遍的概率就更高。

paragraphs.text: Present-day climate change includes both global warming, the ongoing increase in global average temperature, and its wider effects on Earth's climate system. Climate change in a broader sense also includes previous long-term changes to Earth's climate. The modern-day rise in global temperatures is driven by human activities, especially fossil fuel burning since the Industrial Revolution. Fossil fuel use, deforestation, and some agricultural and industrial practices release greenhouse gases. These gases absorb some of the heat that the Earth radiates after it warms from sunlight, warming the lower atmosphere. Earth's atmosphere now has roughly 50% more carbon dioxide, the main gas driving global warming, than it did at the end of the pre-industrial era, reaching levels not seen for millions of years. Climate change has an increasingly large impact on the environment. Heat waves and wildfires are becoming more common. Amplified warming in the Arctic has contributed to thawing permafrost, retreat of glaciers and sea ice decline. Higher temperatures are also causing more intense storms, droughts, and other weather extremes. Rapid environmental change in mountains, coral reefs, and the Arctic is forcing many species to relocate or become extinct. Even if efforts to minimize future warming are successful, some effects will continue for centuries. These include ocean heating, ocean acidification and sea level rise. Climate change threatens people with increased flooding, extreme heat, increased food and water scarcity, more disease, and economic loss. Human migration and conflict can also be a result. The World Health Organization calls climate change one of the biggest threats to global health in the 21st century. Societies and ecosystems will experience more severe risks without action to limit warming. Adapting to climate change through efforts like flood control measures or drought-resistant crops partially reduces climate change risks, although some limits to adaptation have already been reached. Poorer communities are responsible for a small share of global emissions, yet have the least ability to adapt and are most vulnerable to climate change. Many climate change impacts have been observed in the first decades of the 21st century, with 2024 the warmest on record at +1. Additional warming will increase these impacts and can trigger tipping points, such as melting all of the Greenland ice sheet. However, with pledges made under the Agreement, global warming would still reach about 2.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 当今的气候变化既包括全球变暖，即全球平均气温的持续上升，也包括其对地球气候系统更为广泛的影响。广义的气候变化还包括地球气候以往的长期变化。当今全球气温的上升是由人类活动驱动的，尤其是工业革命以来化石燃料的燃烧。化石燃料的使用、森林砍伐以及某些农业和工业活动会排放温室气体。地球受阳光照射升温后会向外辐射热量，这些气体吸收了其中的一部分热量，从而使低层大气变暖。二氧化碳是驱动全球变暖的主要气体，如今地球大气中的二氧化碳比前工业时代末期约高出50%，达到了数百万年来未曾出现的水平。气候变化对环境的影响越来越大。热浪和野火正变得越来越频繁。北极地区被放大的升温，加剧了永久冻土的消融、冰川的退缩和海冰的减少。气温升高还引发了更猛烈的风暴、干旱和其他极端天气。山地、珊瑚礁和北极地区环境的迅速变化，正迫使许多物种迁移他处或走向灭绝。即便尽量减缓未来变暖的努力取得成功，有些影响仍将持续数百年。这些影响包括海洋升温、海洋酸化和海平面上升。气候变化以更频繁的洪涝、极端高温、日益加剧的粮食和水资源短缺、更多疾病以及经济损失威胁着人类。人口迁徙和冲突也可能由此引发。世界卫生组织称气候变化是21世纪全球健康面临的最大威胁之一。若不采取行动限制变暖，社会和生态系统将面临更为严峻的风险。通过防洪措施或抗旱作物等手段适应气候变化，可在一定程度上降低气候变化的风险，尽管适应的某些极限已经达到。较贫困的社区在全球排放中所占份额很小，却最缺乏适应能力，也最易受到气候变化的伤害。21世纪头几十年里，人们已经观测到许多气候变化的影响，2024年成为有记录以来最暖的一年。进一步的变暖会加剧这些影响，并可能触发临界点，例如格陵兰冰盖的全部融化。然而，即便履行《巴黎协定》下作出的各项承诺，全球变暖仍将达到约2摄氏度。

paragraphs.text: Ancient Chinese philosophy encompassed the notion of the universe including both all of space and all of time. Over the centuries, improvements in astronomical observations and theories of motion and gravitation led to ever more accurate descriptions of the universe. The modern era of cosmology began with Albert Einstein's 1915 general theory of relativity, which made it possible to quantitatively predict the origin, evolution, and conclusion of the universe as a whole. Most modern, accepted theories of cosmology are based on general relativity and, more specifically, the predicted Big Bang. Many cultures have stories describing the origin of the world and universe. Cultures generally regard these stories as having some truth. Ethnologists and anthropologists who study myths have developed various classification schemes for the various themes that appear in creation stories. For example, in one type of story, the world is born from a world egg; such stories include the Finnish epic poem Kalevala, the Chinese story of Pangu or the Indian Brahmanda Purana. In other stories, the universe emanates from fundamental principles, such as Brahman and Prakrti, and the creation myth of the Serers. The pre-Socratic Greek philosophers and Indian philosophers developed some of the earliest philosophical concepts of the universe. The earliest Greek philosophers noted that appearances can be deceiving, and sought to understand the underlying reality behind the appearances. In particular, they noted the ability of matter to change forms and several philosophers proposed that all the physical materials in the world are different forms of a single primordial material, or arche. The first to do so was Thales, who proposed this material to be water. Thales' student, Anaximander, proposed that everything came from the limitless apeiron. Anaximenes proposed the primordial material to be air on account of its perceived attractive and repulsive qualities that cause the arche to condense or dissociate into different forms. Anaxagoras proposed the principle of Nous, while Heraclitus proposed fire. Empedocles proposed the elements to be earth, water, air and fire. His four-element model became very popular. Like Pythagoras, Plato believed that all things were composed of number, with Empedocles' elements taking the form of the Platonic solids. Air will immediately rush in to fill a void, and moreover, without resistance, it would do so indefinitely fast. Although Heraclitus argued for eternal change, his contemporary Parmenides emphasized changelessness.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 古代中国哲学中蕴含着这样一种宇宙观念，它既包括所有空间，也包括所有时间。几个世纪以来，天文观测以及运动和引力理论的不断进步，带来了对宇宙越来越精确的描述。现代宇宙学时代始于阿尔伯特·爱因斯坦1915年的广义相对论，该理论使得对整个宇宙的起源、演化和终结进行定量预测成为可能。现代被广泛接受的宇宙学理论大多以广义相对论为基础，更具体地说，是以其所预言的大爆炸为基础。许多文化都有描述世界和宇宙起源的故事。各种文化通常认为这些故事含有某种真理。研究神话的民族学家和人类学家，针对创世故事中出现的各种主题，提出了多种分类方案。例如，在一类故事中，世界从一颗宇宙之卵中诞生；这类故事包括芬兰史诗《卡勒瓦拉》、中国的盘古传说以及印度的《梵卵往世书》。在另一些故事中，宇宙从某些根本本原中生发出来，例如梵与原质，以及塞雷尔人的创世神话。前苏格拉底时期的希腊哲学家和印度哲学家提出了一些最早的关于宇宙的哲学概念。最早的希腊哲学家注意到表象可能具有欺骗性，于是力图理解表象背后的根本实在。他们尤其注意到物质能够变换形态，几位哲学家因此提出，世界上所有的物质材料都是同一种原初物质、即所谓“始基”的不同形态。第一个提出这种看法的是泰勒斯，他认为这种物质就是水。泰勒斯的学生阿那克西曼德则提出，万物都源于无限的“无定”。阿那克西美尼则认为原初物质是气，因为人们觉得气具有吸引和排斥的特性，能使始基凝聚或离散为不同的形态。阿那克萨戈拉提出以“努斯”为本原，而赫拉克利特则提出火为本原。恩培多克勒提出，构成万物的元素是土、水、气和火。他的四元素模型变得非常流行。与毕达哥拉斯一样，柏拉图相信万物都由数构成，而恩培多克勒所说的那些元素则呈现为柏拉图立体的形状。空气会立即涌入以填补虚空，而且在没有阻力的情况下，它会以无限快的速度涌入。尽管赫拉克利特主张永恒的变化，但与他同时代的巴门尼德则强调不变。

paragraphs.text: Democracy is a form of government in which political power is vested in the people or the population of a state. Under a minimalist definition of democracy, rulers are elected through competitive elections while more expansive or maximalist definitions link democracy to guarantees of civil liberties and human rights in addition to competitive elections. In a direct democracy, the people have the direct authority to deliberate and decide legislation. In a representative democracy, the people choose governing officials through elections to do so. The definition of "the people" and the ways authority is shared among them or delegated by them have changed over time and at varying rates in different countries. The notion of democracy has evolved considerably over time. Throughout history, one can find evidence of direct democracy, in which communities make decisions through popular assembly. Today, the dominant form of democracy is representative democracy, where citizens elect government officials to govern on their behalf such as in a parliamentary or presidential democracy. The term appeared in the 5th century BC in Greek city-states, notably Classical Athens, to mean "rule of the people", in contrast to aristocracy, meaning "rule of an elite". In virtually all democratic governments throughout ancient and modern history, democratic citizenship was initially restricted to an elite class, which was later extended to all adult citizens. In most modern democracies, this was achieved through the suffrage movements of the 19th and 20th centuries. Democracy contrasts with forms of government where power is not vested in the general population of a state, such as authoritarian systems. Historically a rare and vulnerable form of government, democratic systems of government have become more prevalent since the 19th century, in particular with various waves of democratization. Democracy garners considerable legitimacy in the modern world, as public opinion across regions tends to strongly favor democratic systems of government relative to alternatives, and as even authoritarian states try to present themselves as democratic. Democracy more consistently results in improved health, education and economic outcomes. At the same time, while representative democracy is widely valued, Pew Research Center found that dissatisfaction with democratic performance is common even in many established democracies. In purely representative democracies, democracy is understood to be defined by free voting, no consensus exists on a precise definition of democracy.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 民主是一种政治权力归于人民、即归于一国全体民众的政府形式。在民主的最简定义下,统治者是通过竞争性选举产生的;而范围更广、即最大化的定义,则在竞争性选举之外,还把民主与对公民自由和人权的保障联系在一起。在直接民主制中,人民拥有直接审议并决定立法的权力。在代议制民主中,人民则通过选举挑选执政官员来代为行使这项权力。人民这一概念的定义,以及权力在人民之间分享或由人民授予的方式,随着时间推移不断变化,而且在不同国家变化的速度各不相同。民主这一概念随着时间推移已发生了很大的演变。纵观历史,人们可以找到直接民主的实例,即各共同体通过民众大会来做出决定。如今,占主导地位的民主形式是代议制民主,公民选举政府官员来代表自己进行治理,议会制民主和总统制民主便是如此。这个词出现于公元前5世纪的希腊城邦,尤其是古典时期的雅典,意为人民的统治,与意为精英统治的贵族制相对。纵观古今,几乎所有民主政体中,民主公民权起初都仅限于精英阶层,后来才扩展到所有成年公民。在大多数现代民主国家,这是通过19世纪和20世纪的选举权运动实现的。民主与权力并不归于一国普通民众的政府形式形成对比,例如威权体制。民主政体在历史上曾是一种罕见而脆弱的政府形式,但自19世纪以来已变得日益普遍,尤其伴随着一波又一波的民主化浪潮。民主在现代世界获得了相当大的正当性,因为各地的民意相比其他选择往往强烈倾向于民主政体,甚至连威权国家也力图把自己塑造成民主国家。民主能更稳定地带来更好的健康、教育和经济成果。与此同时,尽管代议制民主受到广泛重视,但皮尤研究中心发现,即便在许多成熟的民主国家,对民主运作表现的不满也十分普遍。在纯粹的代议制民主中,民主被理解为以自由投票为根本特征,不过对于民主的确切定义,人们至今仍未达成共识。

paragraphs.text: Education is the transmission of knowledge and skills and the development of character traits. Formal education happens in a complex institutional framework, like public schools. Non-formal education is also structured but occurs outside the formal schooling system, while informal education is unstructured learning through daily experiences. Formal and non-formal education are divided into levels that include early childhood education, primary education, secondary education, and tertiary education. Other classifications focus on the teaching method, like teacher-centered and student-centered education, and on the subject, like science education, language education, and physical education. The term "education" can also refer to the mental states and qualities of educated people and the academic field studying educational phenomena. The precise definition of education is disputed, and there are disagreements about what the aims of education are and to what extent education is different from indoctrination by fostering critical thinking. These disagreements affect how to identify, measure, and improve forms of education. Fundamentally, education socializes children into society by teaching cultural values and norms. It equips them with the skills needed to become productive members of society. This way, it stimulates economic growth and raises awareness of local and global problems. Organized institutions affect many aspects of education. For example, governments set education policies to determine when school classes happen, what is taught, and who can or must attend. International organizations, like UNESCO, have been influential in promoting primary education for all children. Many factors influence whether education is successful. Psychological factors include motivation, intelligence, and personality. Social factors, like socioeconomic status, ethnicity, and gender, are often linked to discrimination. Further factors include access to educational technology, teacher quality, and parent involvement. The main academic field investigating education is called education studies. It examines what education is, what aims and effects it has, and how to improve it. Education studies has many subfields, like philosophy, psychology, sociology, anthropology, and economics of education. It also discusses comparative education, pedagogy, and the history of education. In prehistory, education happened informally through oral communication and imitation. With the rise of ancient civilizations, writing was invented, and the amount of knowledge grew. This caused a shift from informal to formal education. Initially, formal education was mainly available to elites and religious groups. The invention of the printing press in the 15th century made books more widely available.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 教育是知识和技能的传递,以及性格特征的培养。正规教育在复杂的制度框架内进行,例如公立学校。非正规教育同样有组织结构,但发生在正规学校体系之外;而非正式教育则是通过日常经验进行的无结构学习。正规教育和非正规教育被划分为若干层次,包括幼儿教育、初等教育、中等教育和高等教育。其他分类方式着眼于教学方法,如以教师为中心和以学生为中心的教育;也着眼于学科,如科学教育、语言教育和体育教育。"教育"一词还可以指受过教育者的心智状态和素养,以及研究教育现象的学术领域。教育的确切定义存在争议,人们对于教育的目的是什么,以及教育在多大程度上因培养批判性思维而区别于灌输,也意见不一。这些分歧影响着人们如何界定、衡量和改进各种教育形式。从根本上说,教育通过传授文化价值观和行为规范,使儿童融入社会。教育使他们具备成为社会有用成员所需的技能。如此一来,教育既促进经济增长,也提高人们对地方性和全球性问题的认识。有组织的机构影响着教育的许多方面。例如,政府制定教育政策,决定学校何时上课、教授什么内容,以及谁可以或必须入学。联合国教育、科学及文化组织等国际组织,在推动让所有儿童接受初等教育方面发挥了重要影响。许多因素影响着教育能否成功。心理因素包括动机、智力和个性。社会因素,如社会经济地位、种族和性别,往往与歧视相关联。其他因素还包括能否获得教育技术、教师素质以及家长的参与程度。研究教育的主要学术领域称为教育学。教育学探讨教育是什么、有何目的和影响,以及如何加以改进。教育学有许多分支领域,如教育哲学、教育心理学、教育社会学、教育人类学和教育经济学。它还涉及比较教育学、教学法和教育史。在史前时期,教育以非正式的方式进行,通过口头交流和模仿来实现。随着古代文明的兴起,文字被发明出来,知识的数量也随之增长。这促成了教育从非正式向正规形式的转变。起初,正规教育主要面向精英和宗教团体。15世纪印刷机的发明,使书籍得以更广泛地普及。

paragraphs.text: In 1958, David Finkelstein identified the Schwarzschild surface as an event horizon, calling it "a perfect unidirectional membrane: causal influences can cross it in only one direction". This means that events that occur inside the black hole cannot affect events that occur outside the black hole. Finkelstein created a new reference frame to include the point of view of infalling observers. Finkelstein's new frame of reference allowed events at the surface of an imploding star to be related to events far away. By 1962 the two points of view were reconciled, convincing many sceptics that implosion into a black hole made physical sense. The era from the mid-1960s to the mid-1970s was the "golden age of black hole research", when general relativity and black holes became mainstream subjects of research. In this period, solutions to the equations of general relativity under various different physical constraints were discovered. In 1963, Roy Kerr found the exact solution for a rotating black hole. Two years later, Ezra Newman found the axisymmetric solution for a black hole that is both rotating and electrically charged. Sciama discovered a series of important mathematical properties of black hole models dubbed "a black hole has no hair" by Wheeler. The first hints came from work by Vitaly Ginzburg who studied a series of increasing compact stars threaded with intense magnetic fields. He discovered that the fields get trapped on the black hole surface. In 1967, Werner Israel showed that any non-spinning, uncharged collapsing star gives a spherically symmetric black hole: any asymmetry must somehow vanish. Price found that the asymmetry was converted into gravitational waves. This view was held in particular by Vladimir Belinski, Isaak Khalatnikov, and Evgeny Lifshitz, who tried to prove that no singularities appear in generic solutions, although they would later reverse their positions. However, in 1965, Roger Penrose proved that general relativity predicts that singularities appear in all black holes, although this may not still hold when quantum mechanics is taken into account. Astronomical observations also made great strides during this era. However, experimental evidence confirming a black hole was very difficult to obtain and ultimately required efforts from many astronomers. X-ray telescope observations by Riccardo Giacconi's team in 1971 showed that Cygnus X-1 emitted x-rays in rapid, sporadic fashion consistent with a compact source.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 1958年，戴维·芬克尔斯坦将史瓦西面认定为事件视界，称它是“一层完美的单向膜：因果影响只能朝一个方向穿过它”。这意味着，黑洞内部发生的事件无法影响黑洞外部发生的事件。芬克尔斯坦建立了一个新的参考系，以纳入向内坠落的观测者的视角。芬克尔斯坦的新参考系使得坍缩恒星表面发生的事件能够与远处发生的事件联系起来。到1962年，这两种观点得到了调和，使许多怀疑者相信坍缩成黑洞在物理上是说得通的。20世纪60年代中期到70年代中期是“黑洞研究的黄金时代”，广义相对论和黑洞在这一时期成为主流的研究课题。在这一时期，人们发现了广义相对论方程在各种不同物理约束下的解。1963年，罗伊·克尔求得了旋转黑洞的精确解。两年后，埃兹拉·纽曼求得了既旋转又带电的黑洞的轴对称解。夏玛发现了黑洞模型的一系列重要数学性质，这些性质被惠勒戏称为“黑洞无毛”。最初的线索来自维塔利·金兹堡的研究，他考察了一系列致密程度不断增加、并贯穿着强磁场的恒星。他发现这些磁场会被束缚在黑洞表面。1967年，沃纳·伊斯雷尔证明，任何不旋转、不带电的坍缩恒星都会形成一个球对称的黑洞：任何不对称都必定会以某种方式消失。普赖斯发现，这种不对称会转化为引力波。这一观点尤其为弗拉基米尔·别林斯基、伊萨克·哈拉特尼科夫和叶夫根尼·栗弗席兹所持有，他们试图证明在一般解中不会出现奇点，尽管后来又改变了立场。然而，1965年，罗杰·彭罗斯证明广义相对论预言所有黑洞中都会出现奇点，尽管在考虑量子力学后这一结论可能不再成立。天文观测在这一时期也取得了长足的进步。然而，确证黑洞存在的实验证据极难获得，最终需要众多天文学家共同努力。1971年，里卡尔多·贾科尼团队的X射线望远镜观测显示，天鹅座X-1以快速而不规则的方式发出X射线，这与一个致密源的特征相符。

paragraphs.text: The reduction of snow cover and sea ice in the Arctic is another major feedback, this reduces the reflectivity of the Earth's surface in the region and accelerates Arctic warming. This additional warming also contributes to permafrost thawing, which releases methane and CO2 into the atmosphere. Around half of human-caused CO2 emissions have been absorbed by land plants and by the oceans. This fraction is not static and if future CO2 emissions decrease, the Earth will be able to absorb up to around 70%. If they increase substantially, it'll still absorb more carbon than now, but the overall fraction will decrease to below 40%. This is because climate change increases droughts and heat waves that eventually inhibit plant growth on land, and soils will release more carbon from dead plants when they are warmer. The rate at which oceans absorb atmospheric carbon will be lowered as they become more acidic and experience changes in thermohaline circulation and phytoplankton distribution. Uncertainty over feedbacks, particularly cloud cover, is the major reason why different climate models project different magnitudes of warming for a given amount of emissions. A climate model is a representation of the physical, chemical and biological processes that affect the climate system. Models include natural processes like changes in the Earth's orbit, historical changes in the Sun's activity, and volcanic forcing. Models are used to estimate the degree of warming future emissions will cause when accounting for the strength of climate feedbacks. Models also predict the circulation of the oceans, the annual cycle of the seasons, and the flows of carbon between the land surface and the atmosphere. The physical realism of models is tested by examining their ability to simulate current or past climates. Past models have underestimated the rate of Arctic shrinkage and underestimated the rate of precipitation increase. Sea level rise since 1990 was underestimated in older models, but more recent models agree well with observations. Additionally, climate models may be unable to adequately predict short-term regional climatic shifts. A subset of climate models add societal factors to a physical climate model. These models simulate how population, economic growth, and energy use affect, and interact with, the physical climate. With this information, these models can produce scenarios of future greenhouse gas emissions. This is then used as input for physical climate models and carbon cycle models to predict how atmospheric concentrations of greenhouse gases might change.
paragraphs.translation_zh: 北极积雪和海冰的减少是另一种重要的反馈，它降低了该地区地表的反射率，并加速了北极的变暖。这种额外的变暖还会加剧永久冻土的融化，从而向大气释放甲烷和二氧化碳。人为产生的二氧化碳排放约有一半被陆地植物和海洋所吸收。这一比例并非一成不变，如果未来二氧化碳排放减少，地球将能够吸收高达约70%的排放。如果排放大幅增加，地球吸收的碳虽然仍会比现在多，但其总体比例会下降到40%以下。这是因为气候变化会加剧干旱和热浪，最终抑制陆地上植物的生长，而土壤温度升高时，会从死亡植物中释放出更多的碳。随着海洋酸性增强，以及热盐环流和浮游植物分布发生变化，海洋吸收大气中碳的速率将会降低。对反馈机制（尤其是云量）的不确定性，是不同气候模型在给定排放量下预测出不同变暖幅度的主要原因。气候模型是对影响气候系统的物理、化学和生物过程的一种表征。这些模型纳入了诸多自然过程，例如地球轨道的变化、太阳活动的历史变化以及火山强迫。在考虑气候反馈强度的情况下，模型被用来估算未来排放将导致的变暖程度。模型还能预测海洋环流、四季的年度循环，以及陆地表面与大气之间的碳流动。模型在物理上的真实性，是通过检验其模拟当前或过去气候的能力来验证的。以往的模型低估了北极冰盖缩减的速率，也低估了降水增加的速率。在较旧的模型中，1990年以来的海平面上升被低估了，但较新的模型与观测结果吻合良好。此外，气候模型可能无法充分预测短期的区域性气候变化。有一部分气候模型在物理气候模型的基础上加入了社会因素。这类模型模拟人口、经济增长和能源使用如何影响物理气候并与之相互作用。借助这些信息，这类模型能够生成未来温室气体排放的各种情景。随后，这些情景被用作物理气候模型和碳循环模型的输入，以预测大气中温室气体浓度可能发生的变化。

paragraphs.text: Examples of traits that can increase fitness are enhanced survival and increased fecundity. Conversely, the lower fitness caused by having a less beneficial or deleterious allele results in this allele likely becoming rarer, they are selected against. Importantly, the fitness of an allele is not a fixed characteristic; if the environment changes, previously neutral or harmful traits may become beneficial and previously beneficial traits become harmful. However, even if the direction of selection does reverse in this way, traits that were lost in the past may not re-evolve in an identical form. "Throwbacks" such as these are known as atavisms. Natural selection within a population for a trait that can vary across a range of values, such as height, can be categorised into three different types. The first is directional selection, which is a shift in the average value of a trait over time, for example, organisms slowly getting taller. Secondly, disruptive selection is selection for extreme trait values and often results in two different values becoming most common, with selection against the average value. This would be when either short or tall organisms had an advantage, but not those of medium height. Finally, in stabilising selection there is selection against extreme trait values on both ends, which causes a decrease in variance around the average value and less diversity. This would, for example, cause organisms to eventually have a similar height. Natural selection most generally makes nature the measure against which individuals and individual traits, are more or less likely to survive. "Nature" in this sense refers to an ecosystem, that is, a system in which organisms interact with every other element, physical as well as biological, in their local environment. Eugene Odum, a founder of ecology, defined an ecosystem as: "Any unit that includes all of the organisms... in a given area interacting with the physical environment so that a flow of energy leads to clearly defined trophic structure, biotic diversity, and material cycles within the system.... " Each population within an ecosystem occupies a distinct niche, or position, with distinct relationships to other parts of the system. These relationships involve the life history of the organism, its position in the food chain and its geographic range.
